metadata.title: Mitigating Resource Usage Dependency in Sorting-based KV Stores on Hybrid Storage Devices via Operation Decoupling
metadata.titleTranslation: 通过操作解耦缓解混合存储设备上基于排序的 KV 存储中的资源使用依赖
metadata.notes: 正文提取范围为 Abstract 至 6 Conclusion；非正文材料已排除。原文中 2 Background and Motivation 与 3 DecouKV Design 后立即进入子节、没有独立正文；为保留全部标题并避免空 section，各自首个子节的第一段被归入对应父 section，文本未重复或改写。

sections.title: Abstract
sections.originalMarkdown:
LSM-tree-based key-value (KV) stores mainly employ sorting-based operations (e.g., flush and compaction) to manage the KV pairs on disk. Through analysis and experiments with RocksDB, we identify that the sorting operations cause critical issues of operation coupling, including intertwined resource consumption within an operation, interdependencies and contention among operations. These coupling problems lead to dependency in resource usage and are particularly exacerbated on hybrid storage devices, causing significant resource fragmentation and increased write stalls. Existing approaches to mitigating write stalls rely on either fixed differentiated data management or superficial scheduling of data sorting operations, but they fail to fundamentally address the resource usage dependency caused by operation coupling.

In this paper, we propose DecouKV, designed to alleviate resource usage dependency and enhance resource utilization on hybrid storage devices through operation decoupling. Specifically, DecouKV decouples data sorting operations into CPU-intensive index merge tasks and I/O-intensive data append and data flush tasks by separating indexes from data files, managing indexes with a mergeable skip list-based structure and managing data with append-only files. Furthermore, we propose an elastic scheme for tuning level capacity and introduce a parameterized queue-based task scheduling strategy to maximize resource utilization. We implement DecouKV and conduct experimental evaluations. Compared to RocksDB, as well as state-of-the-art systems such as MatrixKV, PrismDB, SplitDB and ADOC, DecouKV improves CPU utilization by 25.4%–32.3%, increases throughput by 2.3–4.9×, and reduces tail latency by 74.3%–91.4% under write-intensive workloads. DecouKV also achieves a modest throughput improvement of 1.2–2.3× under read-intensive workloads.
sections.translationMarkdown:
基于 LSM-tree 的键值（KV）存储主要采用基于排序的操作（例如 flush 和 compaction）来管理磁盘上的 KV 对。通过对 RocksDB 的分析和实验，我们发现排序操作会引发严重的操作耦合问题，包括单个操作内部的资源消耗交织、操作之间的相互依赖以及操作之间的竞争。这些耦合问题导致资源使用依赖，并且在混合存储设备上尤为严重，造成显著的资源碎片和更多的写停顿。现有缓解写停顿的方法依赖固定的差异化数据管理或对数据排序操作的表层调度，但无法从根本上解决操作耦合导致的资源使用依赖。

本文提出 DecouKV，旨在通过操作解耦缓解资源使用依赖，并提高混合存储设备上的资源利用率。具体而言，DecouKV 将索引与数据文件分离，使用基于可合并 skip list 的结构管理索引，并使用 append-only file 管理数据，从而把数据排序操作解耦为 CPU 密集型的索引合并任务，以及 I/O 密集型的数据追加和数据 flush 任务。此外，我们提出一种调节层容量的弹性方案，并引入一种基于参数化队列的任务调度策略，以最大化资源利用率。我们实现了 DecouKV 并进行了实验评估。与 RocksDB 以及 MatrixKV、PrismDB、SplitDB 和 ADOC 等最先进的系统相比，在写密集型工作负载下，DecouKV 将 CPU 利用率提高了 25.4%–32.3%，将吞吐量提高了 2.3–4.9×，并将尾延迟降低了 74.3%–91.4%。在读密集型工作负载下，DecouKV 也实现了 1.2–2.3× 的适度吞吐量提升。

sections.title: 1 Introduction
sections.originalMarkdown:
Key-value (KV) stores manage data in the form of KV pairs, enabling fast lookup, insertion, and deletion operations. As a result, KV stores are widely used in distributed systems [1,23], graph storage systems [5,18,31,51], and OLTP/OLAP databases [3, 7, 9, 15, 19, 24, 26, 36, 40, 52]. Modern KV stores typically employ the Log-Structured Merge-tree (LSM-tree) [38], a sorting-based data structure, as their core storage mechanism (e.g., LevelDB [22], RocksDB [20], Cassandra [3], X-Engine [25]). KV pairs are first cached in memory, and then persistently stored on disk through sorting-based flush and compaction operations. By converting random writes into sequential writes, LSM-tree-based KV stores are able to provide efficient write operations and good scalability.

With the continuous advancement of hardware performance, LSM-tree-based KV stores are increasingly deployed on fast devices, such as NVMe SSDs [10, 32, 45] and Persistent Memory (PM) [17, 29, 30, 47], also known as Non-Volatile Memory (NVM). However, these high-performance devices are often expensive [45], leading to the growing popularity of hybrid storage systems that combine fast devices with slower but inexpensive devices such as SATA SSDs and HDDs (e.g., [10, 29, 42, 47]). In such solutions, the common strategy is to store hot data on fast devices while storing large volumes of cold data on slower devices (e.g., [10, 29, 47]). This approach delivers high performance with minimal use of expensive fast devices, ensuring cost efficiency.

LSM-tree and its derivative optimizations [12, 17, 29, 33, 35, 47, 49] primarily adopt a sorting-based multi-level tree structure. These designs rely on heavyweight and unavoidable data sorting operations, such as flush and compaction, to manage KV pairs. However, sorting-based KV stores suffer from significant operation coupling issues, which manifest in three main aspects: (i) both flush and compaction operations consume a certain amount of CPU and I/O resources, leading to intertwined resource consumption; (ii) a sorting operation at a certain level can cause the size of the next level to exceed its threshold and trigger a subsequent sorting operation, resulting in interdependencies among operations; (iii) concurrent two sorting operations compete for system resources, leading to resource contention between these operations. Operation coupling issues lead to severe resource usage dependency, making it difficult to isolate CPU and I/O resources and schedule them effectively. These issues are exacerbated on hybrid storage devices due to the performance disparity between fast and slow devices, which causes fluctuating resource consumption. This results in alternating performance bottlenecks over time and increased write stalls.

Recent related works propose two main strategies for mitigating write stalls. The first strategy leverages the high bandwidth of fast devices to perform differentiated data management across fast and slow devices (e.g., [29, 47]). However, data management is predefined and fixed, which remains misaligned with the fluctuating resource consumption on hybrid storage devices, leading to resource underutilization. The second strategy focuses on superficial scheduling of data sorting operations (e.g., [4, 48]), attempting to reduce latency fluctuations by adjusting the frequency, timing, and placement of sorting operations. Nevertheless, intertwined resource consumption still persists. Superficial scheduling fails to identify the resource-bound nature of sorting operations, and scheduling sorting operations of the same resource-intensive can even exacerbate resource bottlenecks.

To address these issues, we propose DecouKV, which mitigates resource usage dependency in sorting-based KV stores on hybrid storage devices via operation decoupling. DecouKV first decouples the indexes of KV pairs from data files, as in data sorting operations, CPU resource consumption primarily originates from index sorting, while I/O resource consumption is primarily associated with data reading and writing. This decoupling transforms data sorting operations into CPU-intensive index merge tasks and I/O-intensive data append and flush tasks by managing data with append-only files (AOFs) and indexes using a mergeable skip list-based structure called IndexTable, thereby reducing intertwined resource consumption. Additionally, DecouKV introduces two queues, the Index Merge Queue and the Data Flush Queue, to manage IndexTables. By auto-tuning queue parameters, DecouKV proactively schedules decoupled tasks. Specifically, DecouKV minimizes competition between resource-intensive tasks and optimizes resource utilization by coordinating tasks with different resource demands. Finally, DecouKV relaxes level amplification factor limit and uses elastic capacity to reduce interdependencies among data sorting operations.

We implement DecouKV and compare its performance against RocksDB, one of the most widely adopted systems in the industry, as well as two state-of-the-art systems representing the two categories of optimization strategies mentioned before: MatrixKV and ADOC. Experimental results show that under write-intensive workloads, DecouKV improves CPU utilization by 25.4%–32.3%, increases throughput by 2.3–4.9×, and reduces tail latency by 74.3%–91.4%. DecouKV also achieves a modest throughput improvement of 1.2–2.3×, under read-intensive workloads. In summary, our main contributions are summarized as follows.

- We analyze the data sorting operations of sorting-based KV stores and identify operation coupling as the root cause of resource usage dependency and resource fragmentation on hybrid storage devices.
- Based on the analysis above, we propose a decoupling solution, decoupling data sorting operations into CPU-intensive tasks and I/O-intensive tasks to reduce intertwined resource consumption within an operation.
- We design an elastic strategy for tuning the level capacity of LSM-trees, relaxing the level amplification factor to reduce interdependencies among data sorting operations.
- We develop an efficient task scheduling strategy to minimize competition between resource-intensive tasks and optimize resource utilization by coordinating tasks with different resource demands.

The source code of DecouKV is available online [50].
sections.translationMarkdown:
键值（KV）存储以 KV 对的形式管理数据，能够实现快速的查找、插入和删除操作。因此，KV 存储被广泛应用于分布式系统 [1,23]、图存储系统 [5,18,31,51] 以及 OLTP/OLAP 数据库 [3, 7, 9, 15, 19, 24, 26, 36, 40, 52]。现代 KV 存储通常采用 Log-Structured Merge-tree（LSM-tree）[38] 这一基于排序的数据结构作为核心存储机制，例如 LevelDB [22]、RocksDB [20]、Cassandra [3] 和 X-Engine [25]。KV 对首先缓存在内存中，随后通过基于排序的 flush 和 compaction 操作持久化到磁盘。通过将随机写转换为顺序写，基于 LSM-tree 的 KV 存储能够提供高效的写操作和良好的可扩展性。

随着硬件性能不断提高，基于 LSM-tree 的 KV 存储越来越多地部署在高速设备上，例如 NVMe SSD [10, 32, 45] 和 Persistent Memory（PM）[17, 29, 30, 47]，后者也称为 Non-Volatile Memory（NVM）。然而，这些高性能设备通常价格昂贵 [45]，因此，将高速设备与 SATA SSD、HDD 等速度较慢但价格低廉的设备相结合的混合存储系统日益普及（例如 [10, 29, 42, 47]）。在此类方案中，常见策略是将热数据存储在高速设备上，同时将大量冷数据存储在较慢的设备上（例如 [10, 29, 47]）。这种方法只需使用少量昂贵的高速设备即可获得高性能，从而保证成本效益。

LSM-tree 及其衍生优化 [12, 17, 29, 33, 35, 47, 49] 主要采用基于排序的多层树结构。这些设计依赖 flush 和 compaction 等开销高昂且不可避免的数据排序操作来管理 KV 对。然而，基于排序的 KV 存储存在显著的操作耦合问题，主要表现为三个方面：(i) flush 和 compaction 操作都会消耗一定量的 CPU 和 I/O 资源，导致资源消耗相互交织；(ii) 某一层的排序操作可能使下一层的大小超过其阈值，并触发后续排序操作，导致操作之间相互依赖；(iii) 两个并发的排序操作会竞争系统资源，导致这些操作之间发生资源竞争。操作耦合问题会造成严重的资源使用依赖，使 CPU 和 I/O 资源难以隔离和有效调度。由于高速设备与低速设备之间存在性能差异，这些问题在混合存储设备上会进一步加剧，并导致资源消耗发生波动。其结果是性能瓶颈随时间交替出现，写停顿也随之增加。

近期相关工作提出了两种缓解写停顿的主要策略。第一种策略利用高速设备的高带宽，在高速和低速设备之间实施差异化数据管理（例如 [29, 47]）。然而，这种数据管理是预先定义且固定的，仍然无法适应混合存储设备上波动的资源消耗，从而导致资源利用不足。第二种策略侧重于对数据排序操作进行表层调度（例如 [4, 48]），试图通过调整排序操作的频率、时机和位置来减少延迟波动。然而，资源消耗交织的问题依然存在。表层调度无法识别排序操作受何种资源约束，而调度具有相同资源密集属性的排序操作甚至可能加剧资源瓶颈。

为了解决这些问题，我们提出 DecouKV，通过操作解耦缓解混合存储设备上基于排序的 KV 存储中的资源使用依赖。在数据排序操作中，CPU 资源消耗主要来自索引排序，而 I/O 资源消耗主要与数据读写有关，因此 DecouKV 首先将 KV 对的索引与数据文件解耦。DecouKV 使用 append-only file（AOF）管理数据，并使用一种称为 IndexTable 的、基于可合并 skip list 的结构管理索引，从而将数据排序操作转化为 CPU 密集型的索引合并任务以及 I/O 密集型的数据追加和 flush 任务，以减少资源消耗交织。此外，DecouKV 引入 Index Merge Queue 和 Data Flush Queue 两个队列来管理 IndexTable。DecouKV 通过自动调节队列参数，主动调度解耦后的任务。具体而言，DecouKV 通过协调具有不同资源需求的任务，最大限度地减少资源密集型任务之间的竞争并优化资源利用率。最后，DecouKV 放宽层放大因子的限制，并使用弹性容量来减少数据排序操作之间的相互依赖。

我们实现了 DecouKV，并将其性能与业界应用最广泛的系统之一 RocksDB，以及分别代表上述两类优化策略的两个最先进系统 MatrixKV 和 ADOC 进行了比较。实验结果表明，在写密集型工作负载下，DecouKV 将 CPU 利用率提高了 25.4%–32.3%，将吞吐量提高了 2.3–4.9×，并将尾延迟降低了 74.3%–91.4%。在读密集型工作负载下，DecouKV 也实现了 1.2–2.3× 的适度吞吐量提升。总之，我们的主要贡献如下。

- 我们分析了基于排序的 KV 存储中的数据排序操作，并确定操作耦合是混合存储设备上资源使用依赖和资源碎片的根本原因。
- 基于上述分析，我们提出了一种解耦方案，将数据排序操作解耦为 CPU 密集型任务和 I/O 密集型任务，以减少单个操作内部相互交织的资源消耗。
- 我们设计了一种用于调节 LSM-tree 层容量的弹性策略，通过放宽层放大因子来减少数据排序操作之间的相互依赖。
- 我们设计了一种高效的任务调度策略，通过协调具有不同资源需求的任务，最大限度地减少资源密集型任务之间的竞争并优化资源利用率。

DecouKV 的源代码已在线公开 [50]。

sections.title: 2 Background and Motivation
sections.originalMarkdown:
**Architecture of LSM-tree-based KV stores.** As shown in Figure 1, an LSM-tree consists of memory components (MemTables) and disk components organized into Sorted String Tables (SSTables) across multiple levels. Each level has a fixed storage capacity, and the amplification factor between levels is typically set to 10. The system uses background flush and compaction operations, defined here as data sorting operations, to manage data movement between components. These operations are triggered when the component capacity exceeds predefined thresholds. When the MemTable reaches its capacity limit, a flush operation serializes the data (generating metadata like Bloom filters and index blocks) and persists it to disk as SSTables at level 0. When a specific level reaches its capacity limit, the system performs compaction operations, which involve data reading, merge sorting, and data writing. These operations consume significant CPU and I/O resources, leading to resource competition and system performance degradation.

*Figure 1: Architecture of LSM-tree.*
sections.translationMarkdown:
**基于 LSM-tree 的 KV 存储架构。** 如图 1 所示，LSM-tree 由内存组件（MemTable）和磁盘组件组成，磁盘组件以 Sorted String Table（SSTable）的形式组织在多个层中。每一层都有固定的存储容量，层间放大因子通常设置为 10。系统使用后台 flush 和 compaction 操作来管理组件之间的数据移动，本文将这些操作定义为数据排序操作。当组件容量超过预定义阈值时，就会触发这些操作。当 MemTable 达到容量上限时，flush 操作会序列化数据（生成 Bloom filter 和索引块等元数据），并将其作为 level 0 的 SSTable 持久化到磁盘。当某一层达到容量上限时，系统会执行 compaction 操作，其中涉及数据读取、归并排序和数据写入。这些操作消耗大量 CPU 和 I/O 资源，导致资源竞争和系统性能下降。

*图 1：LSM-tree 的架构。*

sections.title: 2.1 KV Stores on Hybrid Storage Devices
sections.titleTranslation: 2.1 混合存储设备上的 KV 存储
sections.originalMarkdown:
**Deployment of LSM-trees on hybrid storage devices.** Recently, LSM-tree-based KV stores have been extended to cost-effective hybrid storage devices, which consist of fast and slow devices. Fast devices, such as NVMe SSDs and Persistent Memory (PM), typically provide high bandwidth and low latency. Although Intel Optane DCPMM [2] has been discontinued, the trend in hardware development continues to prioritize performance improvements (like CXL SSDs [27]). However, their high cost remains a significant barrier—for example, the price of Intel Optane DCPMM per terabyte is more than 10 times higher than that of SATA SSDs [45]. Meanwhile, slower devices such as traditional SSDs and HDDs remain indispensable due to their large capacity and lower cost. Thus, hybrid storage devices become a popular solution offering a balance between performance and cost.

In hybrid devices scenarios, a common approach is to store hot data (e.g., MemTable and lower levels of the LSM-tree) on fast devices (FDs) and cold data (e.g., higher levels) on slow devices (SDs). For example, NoveLSM [29] uses PM as an extension of DRAM and deploys the MemTable on PM. MatrixKV [47] places the level 0 of the LSM-tree on PM through a matrix management approach. SpanDB [10] treats NVMe SSD as a performance device, deploying the low levels of the LSM-tree on it. PrismDB [42] identifies hot and cold data, storing hot data on PM and cold data on slow devices. Overall, by strategically balancing the use of fast and slow devices, these systems achieve high throughput while reducing storage costs.
sections.translationMarkdown:
**LSM-tree 在混合存储设备上的部署。** 近年来，基于 LSM-tree 的 KV 存储已扩展到具有成本效益的混合存储设备上，这类设备由高速设备和低速设备组成。NVMe SSD 和 Persistent Memory（PM）等高速设备通常能够提供高带宽和低延迟。尽管 Intel Optane DCPMM [2] 已经停产，硬件发展趋势仍然优先考虑性能提升（例如 CXL SSD [27]）。然而，高昂的成本仍是一项重大障碍——例如，Intel Optane DCPMM 每 TB 的价格是 SATA SSD 的 10 倍以上 [45]。与此同时，传统 SSD 和 HDD 等较慢的设备因其容量大、成本低，仍然不可或缺。因此，能够在性能与成本之间取得平衡的混合存储设备成为一种流行方案。

在混合设备场景中，一种常见方法是将热数据（例如 MemTable 和 LSM-tree 的较低层）存储在高速设备（FD）上，将冷数据（例如较高层）存储在低速设备（SD）上。例如，NoveLSM [29] 将 PM 用作 DRAM 的扩展，并将 MemTable 部署在 PM 上。MatrixKV [47] 通过矩阵管理方法将 LSM-tree 的 level 0 放置在 PM 上。SpanDB [10] 将 NVMe SSD 视为性能设备，并在其上部署 LSM-tree 的较低层。PrismDB [42] 识别冷热数据，将热数据存储在 PM 上，将冷数据存储在低速设备上。总的来说，通过有策略地平衡高速和低速设备的使用，这些系统在降低存储成本的同时实现了高吞吐量。

sections.title: 2.2 Problems of Operation Coupling
sections.originalMarkdown: Typically, both RocksDB and its derivative optimizations adopt a multi-level, sorting-based design. As mentioned earlier, these designs rely on heavyweight and unavoidable data sorting operations, such as flush and compaction, to manage KV pairs. Below, we first analyze three categories of operation coupling issues. Then, we discuss how these issues exacerbate the underutilization of CPU and I/O resources in sorting-based KV stores deployed on hybrid storage devices, which ultimately results in resource fragmentation and increased write stalls.
sections.translationMarkdown: 通常，RocksDB 及其衍生优化都采用基于排序的多层设计。如前所述，这些设计依赖 flush 和 compaction 等开销高昂且不可避免的数据排序操作来管理 KV 对。下面，我们首先分析三类操作耦合问题。然后，我们讨论这些问题如何加剧部署在混合存储设备上的、基于排序的 KV 存储对 CPU 和 I/O 资源的利用不足，并最终导致资源碎片和更多的写停顿。

sections.title: 2.2.1 Operation Coupling Issues
sections.originalMarkdown:
Specifically, the operation coupling issues in LSM-tree manifest in three key aspects:

**Intertwined resource consumption within an operation.** Data sorting operations require substantial CPU cycles and I/O bandwidth simultaneously. As shown in Figure 2, the serialization (generating metadata like Bloom filters and index blocks) in the flush operation consumes substantial CPU cycles, categorizing it as a CPU-intensive task, whereas data writing primarily consumes I/O bandwidth, classifying it as an I/O-intensive task. Similarly, in the compaction operation, merge sorting is CPU-intensive, while data reading and writing are I/O-intensive. Within a single data sorting operation, the intertwined nature of CPU-intensive and I/O-intensive tasks leads to intertwined resource consumption.

*Figure 2: Intertwined resource consumption within an operation.*

**Interdependencies among operations.** In sorting-based KV stores, data sorting operations are strictly triggered by fixed thresholds, creating dependencies between adjacent levels. As shown in Figure 3, an $L_{i-1}$-to-$L_i$ data sorting operation may increase $L_i$’s size, causing it to exceed its threshold and trigger an $L_i$-to-$L_{i+1}$ data sorting operation. Such interdependencies mean that operations are passively triggered, leaving the system unable to choose the level and timing of data sorting based on resource availability. This results in resource conflicts and waste.

*Figure 3: Interdependencies among operations.*

**Resource contention between operations.** Under high write loads, data sorting operations often occur simultaneously across multiple components of the sorting-based KV stores, leading to competition for system resources. In resource-constrained systems, resource contention reduces the efficiency of sorting operations, causing severe write stalls.
sections.translationMarkdown:
具体而言，LSM-tree 中的操作耦合问题主要表现为以下三个方面：

**单个操作内部的资源消耗交织。** 数据排序操作同时需要大量 CPU 周期和 I/O 带宽。如图 2 所示，flush 操作中的序列化（生成 Bloom filter 和索引块等元数据）消耗大量 CPU 周期，因此属于 CPU 密集型任务；而数据写入主要消耗 I/O 带宽，因此属于 I/O 密集型任务。类似地，在 compaction 操作中，归并排序是 CPU 密集型任务，而数据读取和写入是 I/O 密集型任务。在单个数据排序操作内部，CPU 密集型任务与 I/O 密集型任务相互交织，导致资源消耗相互交织。

*图 2：单个操作内部相互交织的资源消耗。*

**操作之间的相互依赖。** 在基于排序的 KV 存储中，数据排序操作严格由固定阈值触发，从而在相邻层之间形成依赖。如图 3 所示，一次从 $L_{i-1}$ 到 $L_i$ 的数据排序操作可能会增大 $L_i$ 的大小，使其超过阈值，并触发一次从 $L_i$ 到 $L_{i+1}$ 的数据排序操作。这种相互依赖意味着操作是被动触发的，使系统无法根据资源可用情况选择执行数据排序的层和时机。由此会造成资源冲突和浪费。

*图 3：操作之间的相互依赖。*

**操作之间的资源竞争。** 在高写入负载下，基于排序的 KV 存储的多个组件上经常同时发生数据排序操作，从而竞争系统资源。在资源受限的系统中，资源竞争会降低排序操作的效率，造成严重的写停顿。

sections.title: 2.2.2 Exacerbation on Hybrid Storage Devices
sections.titleTranslation: 2.2.2 混合存储设备上的问题加剧
sections.originalMarkdown:
Furthermore, the coupling problem becomes further exacerbated on hybrid storage devices. Due to the performance disparity between fast and slow devices, their capabilities for handling I/O requests differ significantly, leading to varying performance bottlenecks for data sorting operations on different devices. We validate the analysis through experiments with RocksDB deployed on hybrid storage devices.

**Varying bottlenecks on different devices.** First, we measure the percentage of CPU time consumed by data sorting operations on different devices (refer to Section 4 for detailed configurations). Figure 4(a) shows that on fast devices (PM, NVMe SSD), CPU time accounts for over 60% of compaction time, making that the CPU becomes the performance bottleneck for these devices. On slow devices (SATA SSD, HDD), the CPU accounts for less than 30% of the compaction time, with a large proportion of time spent on I/O wait. As a result, I/O bandwidth remains the performance bottleneck for slow devices.

**Alternating bottlenecks on hybrid storage devices.** Next, we test RocksDB’s performance on hybrid storage devices, following the common approach introduced in Section 2.1, where levels 0 and 1 are stored on fast devices (PM) and other levels on slow devices (SATA SSD). We import 100M update requests into a 100GB database, and record sorting times on fast and slow devices, as well as CPU and disk utilization over time. We present a representative 200-second subset, with similar patterns observed throughout the experiment. Figure 4(b) shows that under write-intensive workloads, resource utilization fluctuates significantly, with low averages and severe resource fragmentation. Disk and CPU utilization exhibit opposing patterns: during sorting on fast devices, CPU utilization is high (94%) but disk utilization is low (48%), indicating a CPU bottleneck; during sorting on slow devices, disk utilization is high (88%) while CPU utilization is low (21%), indicating an I/O bottleneck. These bottlenecks alternate between data sorting operations on two devices, hindering the full utilization of CPU and I/O resources and ultimately increasing write stalls.

*Figure 4: Resource utilization of RocksDB on hybrid devices.*

Overall, data sorting operations consume intertwined CPU and I/O resources, and the disparity I/O bandwidth between devices leads to different resource bottlenecks on different devices. Additionally, the fluctuating resource consumption is misaligned with the fixed hardware capabilities. Due to interdependencies among operations, the system cannot proactively trigger operations to utilize resource fragments efficiently (e.g., triggering sorting on fast devices when the CPU is idle).
sections.translationMarkdown:
此外，耦合问题在混合存储设备上会进一步加剧。由于高速和低速设备之间存在性能差异，它们处理 I/O 请求的能力存在显著差别，导致不同设备上的数据排序操作面临不同的性能瓶颈。我们通过在混合存储设备上部署 RocksDB 的实验验证了上述分析。

**不同设备上的瓶颈不同。** 首先，我们测量了数据排序操作在不同设备上消耗的 CPU 时间占比（详细配置参见第 4 节）。图 4(a) 表明，在高速设备（PM、NVMe SSD）上，CPU 时间占 compaction 时间的 60% 以上，使 CPU 成为这些设备的性能瓶颈。在低速设备（SATA SSD、HDD）上，CPU 时间占 compaction 时间的比例不足 30%，大量时间消耗在 I/O 等待上。因此，I/O 带宽仍是低速设备的性能瓶颈。

**混合存储设备上交替出现的瓶颈。** 接下来，我们按照第 2.1 节介绍的常见方法测试 RocksDB 在混合存储设备上的性能：将 level 0 和 level 1 存储在高速设备（PM）上，将其他层存储在低速设备（SATA SSD）上。我们向一个 100GB 数据库中导入 1 亿个更新请求，并记录高速和低速设备上的排序时间，以及 CPU 和磁盘利用率随时间的变化。我们给出了具有代表性的 200 秒时间片段，在整个实验中也观察到了类似模式。图 4(b) 表明，在写密集型工作负载下，资源利用率显著波动，平均值较低，并出现严重的资源碎片。磁盘与 CPU 利用率呈现相反的模式：在高速设备上排序时，CPU 利用率很高（94%），但磁盘利用率较低（48%），表明存在 CPU 瓶颈；在低速设备上排序时，磁盘利用率很高（88%），而 CPU 利用率较低（21%），表明存在 I/O 瓶颈。这些瓶颈在两个设备上的数据排序操作之间交替出现，阻碍了 CPU 和 I/O 资源的充分利用，并最终增加了写停顿。

*图 4：RocksDB 在混合设备上的资源利用率。*

总的来说，数据排序操作会消耗相互交织的 CPU 和 I/O 资源，而设备之间 I/O 带宽的差异会使不同设备出现不同的资源瓶颈。此外，波动的资源消耗与固定的硬件能力并不匹配。由于操作之间存在相互依赖，系统无法主动触发操作来有效利用资源碎片（例如，在 CPU 空闲时触发高速设备上的排序）。

sections.title: 2.3 Limitations of Existing Solutions
sections.titleTranslation: 2.3 现有解决方案的局限性
sections.originalMarkdown:
There are two main strategies from recent related works for mitigating write stalls: (i) fixed differentiated data management and (ii) superficial scheduling of data sorting operations.

**Fixed differentiated data management.** This category of work [10, 29, 42, 47] argues that the performance disparity between fast and slow devices necessitates differentiated management strategies, rather than applying the same approach to both. These works typically modify data sorting operations on specific devices, mitigating intertwined resource consumption to some extent. However, severe interdependencies still exist among the data sorting operations. Data management is predefined and fixed, which remains misaligned with the fluctuating resource consumption on hybrid storage devices, leading to resource underutilization.

Taking MatrixKV [47] as an example, which places L0 of the LSM-tree on persistent memory and employs a matrix container for differentiated management. As shown in Figure 5(a), the amplitude of resource utilization fluctuation is reduced compared to RocksDB (though still exceeding 50%). The result indicates that fine-grained compaction reduces the CPU and I/O resources consumed in each operation, mitigating intertwined resource consumption to some extent. However, the issue of interdependencies among operations persists. Frequent sorting on fast devices keeps CPU utilization at a high level (100–120s in Figure 5(a)). When the data on fast devices exceeds the threshold, a large number of threads shift to sorting on slow devices, causing CPU utilization to drop significantly due to I/O bottlenecks. As a result, the fluctuating resource consumption on hybrid storage causes the average resource utilization to remain low.

**Superficial scheduling of data sorting operations.** Some works [4, 13, 48] aim to improve performance by superficial scheduling data sorting operations. After detecting the system state, they superficially adjust the frequency, timing, and location of these operations to reduce latency fluctuations, alleviating interdependencies among operations to some extent. However, intertwined resource consumption within operation still persists. These works fails to identify the resource-bound nature of sorting operations, as well as the varying performance bottlenecks on hybrid storage devices. Moreover, scheduling sorting operations of the same resource-intensive can even exacerbate resource bottlenecks.

Taking ADOC [48] as an example, which adjusts parameters to control the components and rates at which data sorting operations occur to reduce data blockage. The experiment on hybrid storage devices (Figure 5(b)) shows more pronounced fluctuations in its resource utilization. Compared to RocksDB in Figure 4(b), sorting on fast devices causes a significantly higher CPU utilization, approaching 100%. At the same time, disk utilization decreases significantly, indicating a more severe CPU bottleneck. This occurs because, when data blockage occurs on components in the fast devices, ADOC schedules more sorting threads for sorting on fast devices. Meanwhile, sorting threads on slow devices are reduced. Resource contention between these operations leads to a more severe CPU bottleneck, while the bandwidth of slow devices is underutilized. Conversely, when data blockage occurs on slow devices, ADOC achieves higher disk utilization but lower CPU utilization, indicating a more severe I/O bottleneck. As a result, the bottlenecks significantly alternate due to the intertwined resource consumption within operation.

*Figure 5: Resource utilization fluctuations of existing solutions.*

Although these efforts have made progress in alleviating the write stall issue in LSM-trees, they cannot fundamentally solve the problem of resource fragmentation and underutilization on hybrid storage devices caused by operation coupling. To address this issue comprehensively, decoupling the operations within the sorting-based KV stores is necessary.
sections.translationMarkdown:
近期相关工作主要采用两种策略来缓解写停顿：(i) 固定的差异化数据管理；(ii) 对数据排序操作进行表层调度。

**固定的差异化数据管理。** 这类工作 [10, 29, 42, 47] 认为，高速设备与低速设备之间的性能差异要求采用差异化的管理策略，而不是对二者使用相同的方法。这些工作通常会修改特定设备上的数据排序操作，在一定程度上缓解相互交织的资源消耗。然而，数据排序操作之间仍然存在严重的相互依赖。数据管理是预先定义且固定的，仍然无法适应混合存储设备上波动的资源消耗，从而导致资源利用不足。

以 MatrixKV [47] 为例，它将 LSM-tree 的 L0 放置在 persistent memory 上，并采用矩阵容器进行差异化管理。如图 5(a) 所示，与 RocksDB 相比，资源利用率的波动幅度有所降低（尽管仍超过 50%）。该结果表明，细粒度 compaction 减少了每个操作所消耗的 CPU 和 I/O 资源，从而在一定程度上缓解了资源消耗交织。然而，操作之间的相互依赖问题仍然存在。高速设备上的频繁排序使 CPU 利用率保持在较高水平（图 5(a) 中的 100–120 秒）。当高速设备上的数据超过阈值时，大量线程转而在低速设备上进行排序；由于存在 I/O 瓶颈，CPU 利用率会显著下降。因此，混合存储上波动的资源消耗使平均资源利用率始终较低。

**对数据排序操作进行表层调度。** 一些工作 [4, 13, 48] 旨在通过对数据排序操作进行表层调度来提高性能。在检测系统状态之后，它们从表层调整这些操作的频率、时机和位置，以减少延迟波动，并在一定程度上缓解操作之间的相互依赖。然而，操作内部相互交织的资源消耗依然存在。这些工作无法识别排序操作受何种资源约束，也无法识别混合存储设备上不同的性能瓶颈。此外，调度具有相同资源密集属性的排序操作甚至可能加剧资源瓶颈。

以 ADOC [48] 为例，它通过调整参数来控制发生数据排序操作的组件和速率，以减少数据阻塞。混合存储设备上的实验（图 5(b)）表明，其资源利用率的波动更为明显。与图 4(b) 中的 RocksDB 相比，在高速设备上排序会导致 CPU 利用率显著升高，接近 100%。与此同时，磁盘利用率显著下降，表明 CPU 瓶颈更加严重。这是因为，当高速设备中的组件发生数据阻塞时，ADOC 会调度更多排序线程在高速设备上进行排序，同时减少低速设备上的排序线程。这些操作之间的资源竞争会导致更严重的 CPU 瓶颈，而低速设备的带宽则未得到充分利用。反之，当低速设备发生数据阻塞时，ADOC 的磁盘利用率更高，而 CPU 利用率更低，表明 I/O 瓶颈更加严重。因此，由于操作内部相互交织的资源消耗，瓶颈会显著地交替出现。

*图 5：现有解决方案的资源利用率波动。*

尽管这些工作在缓解 LSM-tree 的写停顿问题方面取得了进展，但它们无法从根本上解决操作耦合所导致的混合存储设备资源碎片和利用不足问题。要全面解决这一问题，就必须对基于排序的 KV 存储内部的操作进行解耦。

sections.title: 3 DecouKV Design
sections.originalMarkdown: DecouKV focuses on decoupling data sorting operations into resource-independent CPU-intensive tasks and I/O-intensive tasks, enabling the system to flexibly schedule tasks based on current resource utilization. The design goals and challenges of DecouKV are as follows:
sections.translationMarkdown: DecouKV 专注于将数据排序操作解耦为资源相互独立的 CPU 密集型任务和 I/O 密集型任务，使系统能够根据当前资源利用率灵活地调度任务。DecouKV 的设计目标和挑战如下：

sections.title: 3.1 Design Goals and Challenges
sections.originalMarkdown:
**Decoupling operations.** DecouKV needs to decouple operations to achieve relative isolation while maintaining correctness and consistency, thereby facilitating subsequent task scheduling and resource utilization. Specifically, DecouKV needs to address the three operation coupling issues discussed in Section 2.2.1.

**Managing decoupled components.** Decoupled operations need to achieve the original objectives of coupled operations. Coupled sorting operations improve data orderliness, addressing two key issues: unordered indexes degrade query performance, and unordered data on disk leads to random access patterns. Decoupled operations must similarly resolve these two issues.

**Efficient scheduling of decoupled tasks.** DecouKV needs to efficiently schedule decoupled tasks to maximize CPU and I/O resource utilization. This requires accurately assessing resource states and making appropriate scheduling decisions to improve resource efficiency.
sections.translationMarkdown:
**解耦操作。** DecouKV 需要对操作进行解耦，在保持正确性和一致性的同时实现相对隔离，从而便于后续的任务调度和资源利用。具体而言，DecouKV 需要解决第 2.2.1 节讨论的三个操作耦合问题。

**管理解耦后的组件。** 解耦后的操作需要实现耦合操作原本要达到的目标。耦合的排序操作通过提高数据有序性来解决两个关键问题：无序索引会降低查询性能，而磁盘上的无序数据会导致随机访问模式。解耦后的操作同样必须解决这两个问题。

**高效调度解耦后的任务。** DecouKV 需要高效地调度解耦后的任务，以最大化 CPU 和 I/O 资源利用率。这要求系统准确评估资源状态，并作出适当的调度决策来提高资源效率。

sections.title: 3.2 System Overview
sections.originalMarkdown:
This section introduces DecouKV, designed to mitigate resource usage dependency in sorting-based KV stores deployed on hybrid devices via operation decoupling. Now, we begin by outlining the architecture of DecouKV.

In sorting-based KV stores, data sorting operations involve CPU-intensive sorting tasks mainly for indexes and I/O-intensive read/write tasks mainly for data. Therefore, to isolate these tasks, DecouKV first decouples indexes from data files. Next, suitable structures are required to manage the separated indexes and data while maintaining their isolation. The structure managing indexes must satisfy the following requirements: (i) fast insertion and query speeds, as these are fundamental for indexes, and (ii) support for independent sorting tasks without impacting access during merging. The structure managing data must meet these criteria: (i) fast insertion speed, and (ii) an unordered design to avoid excessive CPU consumption for data sorting. As shown in Figure 6, DecouKV manages data on fast devices using append-only files (AOFs) and manages their indexes in DRAM using IndexTables, a mergeable structure based on skip list.

**Figure 6:** DecouKV overview.

In this way, DecouKV decouples data sorting operations into three types of tasks: CPU-intensive index merge tasks and I/O-intensive data append and data flush tasks, reducing intertwined resource consumption. Specifically, index merge tasks focus solely on sorting indexes in DRAM without involving data read/write operations, making them CPU-intensive. Data append and flush tasks primarily consume disk I/O without sorting, making them I/O-intensive.

Additionally, DecouKV employs two DRAM-based queues—Index Merge Queue (IMQ) and Data Flush Queue (DFQ)—to manage IndexTables. By autotuning queue parameters, DecouKV actively schedules the decoupled tasks. Specifically, DecouKV minimizes competition among the same resource-intensive tasks and optimizes resource usage by coordinating tasks with different resource demands. Finally, DecouKV introduces elastic capacity design for high levels (HLs) of the LSM-tree on slow devices, relaxing the strict level amplification factor to reduce interdependencies among operations. The subsequent sections detail the design of these decoupling mechanisms.
sections.translationMarkdown:
本节介绍 DecouKV。DecouKV 旨在通过操作解耦，缓解部署在混合设备上的基于排序的 KV store 中的资源使用依赖问题。下面首先概述 DecouKV 的架构。

在基于排序的 KV store 中，数据排序操作包含主要面向索引的 CPU 密集型排序任务，以及主要面向数据的 I/O 密集型读写任务。因此，为了隔离这些任务，DecouKV 首先将索引与数据文件解耦。接下来，需要使用合适的结构分别管理拆分后的索引和数据，同时维持二者之间的隔离。管理索引的结构必须满足以下要求：(i) 具备快速的插入与查询速度，因为这些是索引的基本要求；(ii) 支持独立的排序任务，并且合并期间不影响访问。管理数据的结构则必须满足以下条件：(i) 插入速度快；(ii) 采用无序设计，以避免数据排序消耗过多 CPU。如图 6 所示，DecouKV 使用 append-only file（AOF）管理快速设备上的数据，并使用 IndexTable 在 DRAM 中管理相应索引；IndexTable 是一种基于 skip list 的可合并结构。

**图 6：** DecouKV 概览。

通过这种方式，DecouKV 将数据排序操作解耦为三类任务：CPU 密集型的索引合并任务，以及 I/O 密集型的数据追加和数据刷写任务，从而减少相互交织的资源消耗。具体而言，索引合并任务只负责在 DRAM 中对索引进行排序，不涉及数据读写操作，因此属于 CPU 密集型任务。数据追加和刷写任务主要消耗磁盘 I/O 而不执行排序，因此属于 I/O 密集型任务。

此外，DecouKV 使用两个基于 DRAM 的队列——Index Merge Queue（IMQ）和 Data Flush Queue（DFQ）——来管理 IndexTable。通过自动调优队列参数，DecouKV 主动调度解耦后的任务。具体而言，DecouKV 尽量减少对同类资源需求密集的任务之间的竞争，并通过协调资源需求不同的任务来优化资源使用。最后，DecouKV 为慢速设备上 LSM-tree 的高层（high level，HL）引入弹性容量设计，通过放宽严格的层级放大因子来降低操作之间的相互依赖。后续各节将详细介绍这些解耦机制的设计。

sections.title: 3.3 Decoupled Components
sections.originalMarkdown:
DecouKV manages indexes with DRAM-based IndexTables and data with AOFs. Below, we describe their structures.

**IndexTable.** DecouKV employs IndexTables, a mergeable skip list-based structure, to store indexes in DRAM (Figure 6). Each IndexTable uses a skip list to manage indexes, enabling efficient lookups and subsequent index merge tasks. DecouKV leverages the index to precisely locate data in fast devices, with each index entry containing a key and the corresponding data address in fast devices (comprising an 8-byte file number and an 8-byte offset). Unlike RocksDB’s MemTable, IndexTables avoid storing values, supporting more entries within the same memory footprint. To ensure efficient index insertion and memory utilization, the initial capacity of the IndexTable, denoted as *max_index_size*, is set to a relatively small value (8 MB). Note that to minimize the impact of I/O operations on the indexes after decoupling, DecouKV employs asynchronous insertion. By recording the data address, DecouKV allows insertion to proceed without waiting for the data append to fast devices to complete. Once the IndexTable reaches *max_index_size*, it becomes non-insertable, and a new IndexTable is created. Queries traverse IndexTables sequentially, from newest to oldest, until the required key is located. When the number of IndexTables exceeds a threshold, DecouKV triggers an index merge operation, which will be discussed in the next subsection.

**Append-only file (AOF).** DecouKV stores KV pairs as AOFs on fast devices, with the corresponding indexes in DRAM-based IndexTables. Although AOFs are unordered files prone to random access, DecouKV leverages the high bandwidth and concurrency of fast devices to achieve random access performance comparable to sequential access. Consequently, AOFs maintain unordered data without compromising performance. Furthermore, the original Write-Ahead Logs (WALs) of the LSM-tree are eliminated as DecouKV can recover data with AOFs during system failures. Additionally, AOFs also eliminate the extra CPU overhead associated with generating Bloom filters for the data on fast devices, and reduce the memory overhead required for caching these Bloom filters during query processing. Because of the excellent random access performance of fast storage devices, the impact on read performance caused by removing the Bloom Filter in AOF is effectively mitigated.
sections.translationMarkdown:
DecouKV 使用基于 DRAM 的 IndexTable 管理索引，并使用 AOF 管理数据。下面介绍它们的结构。

**IndexTable。** DecouKV 使用 IndexTable（一种基于 skip list 的可合并结构）在 DRAM 中存储索引（图 6）。每个 IndexTable 都使用一个 skip list 管理索引，从而支持高效查找和后续的索引合并任务。DecouKV 利用索引精确定位快速设备中的数据；每个索引项包含一个 key 以及该 key 对应的数据在快速设备中的地址，该地址由一个 8-byte 文件编号和一个 8-byte 偏移量组成。与 RocksDB 的 MemTable 不同，IndexTable 不存储 value，因此能在相同的内存占用下容纳更多条目。为了保证索引插入和内存利用的效率，IndexTable 的初始容量（记为 *max_index_size*）被设置为一个相对较小的值（8 MB）。需要注意的是，为了尽量降低解耦后 I/O 操作对索引的影响，DecouKV 采用异步插入。通过记录数据地址，DecouKV 无需等待向快速设备追加数据的操作完成，即可继续进行插入。当 IndexTable 达到 *max_index_size* 后，它将不再允许插入，同时系统会创建一个新的 IndexTable。查询按照从新到旧的顺序依次遍历 IndexTable，直到找到所需的 key。当 IndexTable 的数量超过阈值时，DecouKV 会触发索引合并操作，该操作将在下一小节讨论。

**Append-only file（AOF）。** DecouKV 将 KV pair 以 AOF 形式存储在快速设备上，并将其对应索引存储在基于 DRAM 的 IndexTable 中。虽然 AOF 是容易产生随机访问的无序文件，但 DecouKV 利用快速设备的高带宽和高并发能力，使随机访问性能达到与顺序访问相当的水平。因此，AOF 可以在不损害性能的情况下保持数据无序。此外，由于系统发生故障时 DecouKV 可以使用 AOF 恢复数据，因此移除了 LSM-tree 原有的 Write-Ahead Log（WAL）。AOF 还消除了为快速设备上的数据生成 Bloom filter 所产生的额外 CPU 开销，并减少了查询处理期间缓存这些 Bloom filter 所需的内存开销。得益于快速存储设备出色的随机访问性能，移除 AOF 中的 Bloom Filter 对读取性能造成的影响得到了有效缓解。

sections.title: 3.4 Decoupled Tasks
sections.originalMarkdown:
Based on IndexTables and AOFs, DecouKV decouples data sorting operations into CPU-intensive index merge tasks and I/O-intensive data append and data flush tasks. In the following, we explain how to handle these decoupled tasks to achieve the objectives of the original coupled tasks outlined in Section 3.1.

**Index merge.** Obviously, the number of IndexTables directly impacts query performance. To address the first issue mentioned in Section 3.1—unordered indexes degrade query performance—index merge tasks reduce the number of unordered IndexTables. As illustrated in Figure 7, DecouKV employs index merge tasks to consolidate two red IndexTables into a larger one. Note that index merge tasks occur entirely in DRAM without affecting the data in AOFs. Therefore, index merge is a CPU-intensive task. Initially, each IndexTable corresponds to one AOF; after merging, one IndexTable may correspond to multiple AOFs.

**Figure 7:** Index merge. The red cylinders represent the IndexTables before and after merging, and the arrows indicate the correspondence between the IndexTables and AOFs.

Index merge effectively merges two skip lists. Recent studies have introduced many optimizations for merging skip list structures [17,30]. Specifically, by altering the pointers within skip lists, the two skip lists can be merged efficiently without blocking query requests. DecouKV further implements multithreaded parallelism based on these optimizations, making full use of CPU resources. As shown in Figure 8, DecouKV uses multiple threads to perform parallel index merge. In Figure 8(b), the red arrows are pointers that are currently being modified. Meanwhile, DecouKV maintains a Merging Index Set (a lock-free set implemented using CAS) to store atomic pointers to the index nodes being merged (3 and 8 in the figure). In this way, after accessing IndexTable 0, the query operation will first check the Merging Index Set and then access IndexTable 1 to ensure the correctness of the query. Once the node insertion is complete, the thread removes the corresponding pointer from the Merging Index Set.

**Figure 8:** Illustration of multithreaded parallel index merge. The figure shows two threads performing parallel merging. The arrows represent skip list pointers.

**Data append and data flush.** DecouKV employs the data append task, an I/O-intensive operation, to write KV pairs into the AOFs on fast devices. Additionally, DecouKV performs the data flush task to flush KV pairs into slow devices. Notably, to avoid the performance gap between random and sequential access on slow devices, DecouKV organizes KV pairs in an ordered data structure (SSTables) on slow devices. When the size of a merged IndexTable reaches the threshold *data_flush_trigger_size*, DecouKV triggers a data flush operation to consolidate the IndexTable and its corresponding AOFs into the slow devices. Specifically, the data flush task sequentially scans all indexes in the IndexTable, retrieves the data from the AOFs and writes them into the SSTables on slow devices. Finally, the system deletes the outdated IndexTable and its corresponding AOFs. Note that while the data flush task inevitably introduces some CPU overhead, I/O remains the primary performance bottleneck on slow devices. Furthermore, since the indexes in the IndexTable are already organized as sorted skip lists, the data flush task is still predominantly classified as an I/O-intensive operation.
sections.translationMarkdown:
基于 IndexTable 和 AOF，DecouKV 将数据排序操作解耦为 CPU 密集型的索引合并任务，以及 I/O 密集型的数据追加和数据刷写任务。下面说明如何处理这些解耦后的任务，从而实现第 3.1 节所述的原有耦合任务的目标。

**索引合并。** 显然，IndexTable 的数量会直接影响查询性能。为了解决第 3.1 节提到的第一个问题——无序索引会降低查询性能——索引合并任务会减少无序 IndexTable 的数量。如图 7 所示，DecouKV 通过索引合并任务将两个红色 IndexTable 合并为一个更大的 IndexTable。需要注意的是，索引合并任务完全在 DRAM 中进行，不会影响 AOF 中的数据。因此，索引合并属于 CPU 密集型任务。最初，每个 IndexTable 对应一个 AOF；合并后，一个 IndexTable 可能对应多个 AOF。

**图 7：** 索引合并。红色圆柱表示合并前后的 IndexTable，箭头表示 IndexTable 与 AOF 之间的对应关系。

索引合并本质上是对两个 skip list 进行合并。近期研究已经针对 skip list 结构的合并提出了许多优化 [17,30]。具体而言，通过改变 skip list 内部的指针，可以在不阻塞查询请求的情况下高效合并两个 skip list。DecouKV 在这些优化的基础上进一步实现了多线程并行，从而充分利用 CPU 资源。如图 8 所示，DecouKV 使用多个线程并行执行索引合并。在图 8(b) 中，红色箭头表示当前正在修改的指针。同时，DecouKV 维护一个 Merging Index Set（使用 CAS 实现的无锁集合），用于存储指向正在合并的索引节点的原子指针（图中的 3 和 8）。采用这种方式后，查询操作在访问 IndexTable 0 之后，会先检查 Merging Index Set，然后再访问 IndexTable 1，以确保查询的正确性。当节点插入完成后，线程会从 Merging Index Set 中移除相应的指针。

**图 8：** 多线程并行索引合并示意图。图中展示了两个线程并行执行合并的过程。箭头表示 skip list 指针。

**数据追加和数据刷写。** DecouKV 使用数据追加任务这种 I/O 密集型操作，将 KV pair 写入快速设备上的 AOF。此外，DecouKV 还执行数据刷写任务，将 KV pair 刷写到慢速设备中。值得注意的是，为了避免慢速设备上随机访问和顺序访问之间的性能差距，DecouKV 在慢速设备上使用有序数据结构（SSTable）来组织 KV pair。当一个已合并 IndexTable 的大小达到阈值 *data_flush_trigger_size* 时，DecouKV 会触发数据刷写操作，将该 IndexTable 及其对应的 AOF 整合到慢速设备中。具体而言，数据刷写任务会顺序扫描 IndexTable 中的所有索引，从 AOF 中取出数据，并将其写入慢速设备上的 SSTable。最后，系统删除过时的 IndexTable 及其对应的 AOF。需要注意的是，虽然数据刷写任务不可避免地会引入一些 CPU 开销，但 I/O 仍然是慢速设备上的主要性能瓶颈。此外，由于 IndexTable 中的索引已经组织为有序 skip list，数据刷写任务仍主要被归类为 I/O 密集型操作。

sections.title: 3.5 Scheduling Strategy of Decoupled Tasks
sections.originalMarkdown:
In this section, we discuss the scheduling of decoupled CPU-intensive and I/O-intensive tasks to maximize system resource utilization. DecouKV employs two distinct queues to evaluate system resource status and uses corresponding parameters to manage task scheduling effectively.

**Index Merge Queue (IMQ).** DecouKV enqueues small non-insertable IndexTables into the IMQ, awaiting index merge. Since index merge is a CPU-intensive task, the length of the IMQ ($L_{IMQ}$) reflects the availability of CPU resources. When CPU resources become bottlenecked, the number of IndexTables waiting for merge increases, causing the IMQ to grow. The threshold for triggering an index merge task is referred to as *index_merge_trigger_num* (IMTN). We define

$$Score_{IM}=\frac{L_{IMQ}}{IMTN}$$

to represent CPU resource pressure. When $Score_{IM}$ exceeds a certain value (1.5 is optimal in our experiments), DecouKV identifies the system as CPU-bound.

DecouKV can adjust CPU resource demand by modifying the IMTN parameter. By increasing IMTN, the IMQ can accommodate more IndexTables, thereby reducing the frequency of index merge triggers and alleviating the pressure on CPU resources. Conversely, decreasing IMTN increases the demand for CPU resources.

**Data Flush Queue (DFQ).** For IndexTables that reach the threshold size of *data_flush_trigger_size* (DFTS), DecouKV places them into the DFQ awaiting data flush. Similarly, the length of the DFQ ($L_{DFQ}$) reflects the pressure of I/O resources. Since data flush is an I/O-intensive task, when I/O resources become bottlenecked, the DFQ length increases. We define

$$Score_{DF}=L_{DFQ}$$

to represent I/O resource pressure. When $Score_{DF}$ exceeds a certain value (3 is optimal in our experiments), DecouKV identifies the system as I/O-bound.

DecouKV adjusts the DFTS to schedule different resource-intensive tasks. As shown in Figure 9, increasing the DFTS causes some IndexTables to move from the DFQ back to the IMQ, thereby increasing CPU demand and alleviating I/O pressure. Conversely, decreasing the DFTS alleviates CPU pressure and increases I/O demand.

**Figure 9:** Auto-tuning DFTS. The height of the cylinder represents the size of the IndexTable. The yellow cylinder is the IndexTable that has been moved during auto-tuning DFTS.
sections.translationMarkdown:
本节讨论如何调度解耦后的 CPU 密集型任务和 I/O 密集型任务，以最大化系统资源利用率。DecouKV 使用两个不同的队列来评估系统资源状态，并使用相应参数有效地管理任务调度。

**Index Merge Queue（IMQ）。** DecouKV 将较小且不可再插入的 IndexTable 加入 IMQ，等待执行索引合并。由于索引合并属于 CPU 密集型任务，因此 IMQ 的长度（$L_{IMQ}$）反映了 CPU 资源的可用情况。当 CPU 资源成为瓶颈时，等待合并的 IndexTable 数量会增加，导致 IMQ 变长。触发索引合并任务的阈值称为 *index_merge_trigger_num*（IMTN）。我们定义

$$Score_{IM}=\frac{L_{IMQ}}{IMTN}$$

来表示 CPU 资源压力。当 $Score_{IM}$ 超过某个值时（在我们的实验中，最优值为 1.5），DecouKV 将系统判定为 CPU-bound。

DecouKV 可以通过修改 IMTN 参数来调整 CPU 资源需求。增大 IMTN 后，IMQ 可以容纳更多 IndexTable，从而降低索引合并的触发频率并缓解 CPU 资源压力。反之，减小 IMTN 会增加对 CPU 资源的需求。

**Data Flush Queue（DFQ）。** 对于大小达到 *data_flush_trigger_size*（DFTS）阈值的 IndexTable，DecouKV 会将其放入 DFQ，等待执行数据刷写。类似地，DFQ 的长度（$L_{DFQ}$）反映 I/O 资源的压力。由于数据刷写属于 I/O 密集型任务，当 I/O 资源成为瓶颈时，DFQ 的长度会增加。我们定义

$$Score_{DF}=L_{DFQ}$$

来表示 I/O 资源压力。当 $Score_{DF}$ 超过某个值时（在我们的实验中，最优值为 3），DecouKV 将系统判定为 I/O-bound。

DecouKV 通过调整 DFTS 来调度对不同资源需求密集的任务。如图 9 所示，增大 DFTS 会使部分 IndexTable 从 DFQ 移回 IMQ，从而增加 CPU 需求并缓解 I/O 压力。反之，减小 DFTS 会缓解 CPU 压力并增加 I/O 需求。

**图 9：** DFTS 自动调优。圆柱的高度表示 IndexTable 的大小。黄色圆柱表示在 DFTS 自动调优期间被移动的 IndexTable。

sections.title: 3.6 Elastic Capacity High Levels
sections.originalMarkdown:
For the high levels (HLs) stored on slow devices, DecouKV still uses leveled compaction. Due to memory limitations and the random access performance of slow devices, we cannot leverage the same decoupling design as lower levels. Therefore, we chose to use elastic capacity to mitigate the coupling issue at high levels by relaxing the strict amplification ratio between levels. In Section 2.2, we mentioned three key aspects of the operation coupling issue, with the second point discussing how interdependencies among operations stem from fixed thresholds. Elastic capacity is mainly optimized to address this problem. For example, a large number of $HL_0$ compactions may lead to an IO-bound situation, which also causes $HL_1$ to exceed its threshold and trigger further compaction, leading to an even worse IO-bound situation. In this case, DecouKV relaxes the capacity threshold for $HL_1$, postpones the sorting for that part, and schedules CPU-intensive index merges to improve CPU utilization.

However, relaxing the amplification ratio between levels in other KV stores may temporarily lead to increased size in certain levels, potentially introducing some challenges. (i) Increased write amplification: When the size of $L_i$ increases while the size of $L_{i-1}$ remains relatively smaller, the compaction between $L_{i-1}$ and $L_i$ will involve more data from $L_i$, causing additional write amplification. (ii) Higher read overhead: With the larger size of $L_i$, the cost of read operations accessing $L_i$ will increase.

Fortunately, the decoupling design in DecouKV, as described in Section 3.3, successfully addresses both challenges. For the first one, the DFTS parameter (which directly influences the file size written to $HL_0$) is adjustable. This allows DecouKV to control the size of files written to $HL_0$. By adjusting the capacity of the high levels according to DFTS, DecouKV ensures that the size ratio between adjacent levels does not become excessively large, thereby controlling write amplification. Additionally, the use of the index merge operation reduces some of the write amplification caused by duplicate keys. For the second one, DecouKV stores hotter data in fast devices, while cold data in the high levels is accessed less frequently. Moreover, data written to higher levels is pre-sorted, resulting in larger sizes for high levels but fewer overall levels. Subsequent experiments demonstrate that even under read-intensive workloads, DecouKV achieves a slight improvement in throughput.
sections.translationMarkdown:
对于存储在慢速设备上的高层（HL），DecouKV 仍然采用 leveled compaction。由于内存限制以及慢速设备的随机访问性能，我们无法在这些高层使用与低层相同的解耦设计。因此，我们选择使用弹性容量，通过放宽层与层之间严格的放大比例来缓解高层的耦合问题。在第 2.2 节中，我们提到了操作耦合问题的三个关键方面，其中第二点讨论了操作之间的相互依赖如何源自固定阈值。弹性容量主要就是针对这一问题进行优化。例如，大量 $HL_0$ compaction 可能使系统进入 IO-bound 状态，这也会导致 $HL_1$ 超过其阈值并触发进一步的 compaction，使 IO-bound 状态进一步恶化。在这种情况下，DecouKV 会放宽 $HL_1$ 的容量阈值，推迟对这一部分进行排序，并调度 CPU 密集型的索引合并，以提高 CPU 利用率。

然而，在其他 KV store 中放宽层与层之间的放大比例，可能会暂时增大某些层的大小，并带来一些潜在挑战。(i) 写放大增加：当 $L_i$ 的大小增大，而 $L_{i-1}$ 的大小仍相对较小时，$L_{i-1}$ 与 $L_i$ 之间的 compaction 会涉及 $L_i$ 中更多的数据，从而产生额外的写放大。(ii) 读取开销增大：随着 $L_i$ 变大，读取操作访问 $L_i$ 的成本也会增加。

幸运的是，第 3.3 节所述的 DecouKV 解耦设计成功解决了这两个挑战。对于第一个挑战，DFTS 参数是可调的，而该参数会直接影响写入 $HL_0$ 的文件大小。这使 DecouKV 能够控制写入 $HL_0$ 的文件大小。DecouKV 根据 DFTS 调整高层容量，确保相邻层之间的大小比例不会变得过大，从而控制写放大。此外，索引合并操作还减少了由重复 key 引起的部分写放大。对于第二个挑战，DecouKV 将较热的数据存储在快速设备中，而位于高层的冷数据被访问得更少。此外，写入更高层的数据已经预先排好序，因此高层虽然更大，但系统的总层数更少。后续实验表明，即使在读取密集型 workload 下，DecouKV 的吞吐量也略有提升。

sections.title: 3.7 Auto-tuning for Task Scheduling
sections.titleTranslation: 3.7 面向任务调度的自动调优
sections.originalMarkdown:
As shown in Figure 10, first, DecouKV monitors the IMQ and DFQ to assess the system resource state. It then determines whether the system is in a resource-constrained state based on the scores defined in Section 3.5. Following this, DecouKV performs auto-tuning according to the actual system state. By adjusting IMTN, DFTS, and the level capacity, DecouKV schedules resource-intensive tasks accordingly. This ensures efficient utilization of system resources and reduces fragmentation. Detailed parameter settings and tuning methods are presented below.

**Figure 10:** Control flow of task scheduling.

The parameter setting in DecouKV is based on the default values of similar parameters in RocksDB. As a widely adopted industrial storage engine, the parameters in RocksDB are fine-tuned through numerous experimental optimizations. We also validate the efficiency of the selected values via experiments. Specifically, the default value of IMTN in our evaluation is 2. Since IMTN directly impacts read performance, we set it according to the default number of MemTables, which similarly affects read performance. The default value of DFTS is 32MB (4 * 8MB), which is approximately the size of 4 merged IndexTables according to the number of files in $L_0$ (default 4). When the system is CPU-bound, DecouKV increases the IMTN to reduce CPU resource demand. Simultaneously, DecouKV decreases the DFTS to minimize the triggering of CPU-intensive index merge operations while scheduling more I/O-intensive data flush tasks. Conversely, when the system is I/O-bound, DecouKV reduces the IMTN and increases the DFTS to lower I/O resource demand, thereby scheduling more CPU-intensive tasks. Additionally, proportionally adjusting the capacity of the high levels based on DFTS further regulates I/O resource pressure on slow devices. Note that DecouKV adjusts IMTN by increasing 2 (or decreasing 2) and adjust DFTS by multiplying (or dividing) by 2 according to the state, since the number of IndexTables typically increases incrementally, while the data size often increases exponentially (e.g., two IndexTables of 16MB each are merged into a 32MB IndexTable).

Naturally, these parameters are capped and cannot grow indefinitely. If both CPU and I/O are bound (indicated by congestion in both IMQ and DFQ), DecouKV recognizes that the incoming data writes exceeds the system’s processing capacity and slows down data write through write stalls. Finally, when neither queue experiences congestion over a certain period (set to 60 seconds in our implementation), DecouKV identifies that system resources are idle. In this case, it reduces the IMTN, DFTS, and the capacity of the high levels simultaneously to schedule both CPU- and I/O-intensive tasks effectively. In this way, DecouKV improves data orderliness to enhance read performance.
sections.translationMarkdown:
如图 10 所示，DecouKV 首先监控 IMQ 和 DFQ，以评估系统的资源状态。随后，它根据第 3.5 节定义的分数判断系统是否处于资源受限状态。接着，DecouKV 根据系统的实际状态进行自动调优。通过调整 IMTN、DFTS 和层容量，DecouKV 对资源密集型任务进行相应调度。这能够确保高效利用系统资源，并减少碎片。下面给出详细的参数设置和调优方法。

**图 10：** 任务调度的控制流程。

DecouKV 的参数设置基于 RocksDB 中类似参数的默认值。RocksDB 是一种被广泛采用的工业级存储引擎，其参数已经通过大量实验优化进行精细调优。我们也通过实验验证了所选参数值的有效性。具体而言，在我们的评估中，IMTN 的默认值为 2。由于 IMTN 会直接影响读取性能，因此我们根据 MemTable 的默认数量来设置该参数；MemTable 数量同样会影响读取性能。DFTS 的默认值为 32MB（4 * 8MB），根据 $L_0$ 中的文件数量（默认为 4），这大约等于 4 个合并后 IndexTable 的大小。当系统处于 CPU-bound 状态时，DecouKV 增大 IMTN，以降低 CPU 资源需求。同时，DecouKV 减小 DFTS，以尽量避免触发 CPU 密集型的索引合并操作，同时调度更多 I/O 密集型的数据刷写任务。相反，当系统处于 I/O-bound 状态时，DecouKV 减小 IMTN 并增大 DFTS，以降低 I/O 资源需求，从而调度更多 CPU 密集型任务。此外，根据 DFTS 按比例调整高层容量，可以进一步调节慢速设备上的 I/O 资源压力。需要注意的是，DecouKV 会根据系统状态将 IMTN 增加 2（或减少 2），并将 DFTS 乘以 2（或除以 2）；这是因为 IndexTable 的数量通常线性增长，而数据大小通常指数增长（例如，两个大小分别为 16MB 的 IndexTable 会合并成一个 32MB 的 IndexTable）。

当然，这些参数都有上限，不能无限增大。如果 CPU 和 I/O 都成为瓶颈（表现为 IMQ 和 DFQ 同时拥塞），DecouKV 会判断传入数据的写入速率已经超过系统的处理能力，并通过 write stall 降低数据写入速度。最后，如果两个队列在一段时间内都没有发生拥塞（在我们的实现中设置为 60 秒），DecouKV 会判定系统资源处于空闲状态。在这种情况下，它会同时减小 IMTN、DFTS 和高层容量，从而有效调度 CPU 密集型任务和 I/O 密集型任务。通过这种方式，DecouKV 提高了数据有序性，从而改善读取性能。

sections.title: 4 Evaluation
sections.originalMarkdown:
In this section, we evaluate and compare DecouKV with RocksDB v9.3.0 and the two categories of solutions mentioned in 2.3 using hybrid storage devices. Among the works following the fixed differentiated management approach, MatrixKV stands out as a typical representative. In the superficial scheduling direction, ADOC represents the state-of-the-art. Therefore, we select RocksDB, MatrixKV and ADOC as the basic benchmarks for comparison. Besides, some state-of-the-art works on hybrid storage, such as PrismDB [42] and SplitDB [6], mainly focus on different scenarios like workload skewness or read-intensive workloads. To ensure a comprehensive evaluation, we also include them in our comparative analysis under YCSB workloads. The test data are obtained from three experimental runs, with minimal variation in the results. The average values are presented.

**Hardware environment.** The experiments are conducted on a server equipped with two 20-core Intel Xeon Gold 5218R processors and 128 GB of DRAM. The server runs Ubuntu 20.04.6 LTS with the Linux kernel 5.15. For storage devices, we use 128 GB Intel Optane DCPMM and INTEL SSDPE2KE032T8 NVMe SSDs for fast devices, and 960 GB Intel S4520 SSDs with SATA 3.0 interfaces for slow devices.

**Configuration of key-value stores.** For all KV stores, we use the recommended configuration [21], setting the Memtable size and SSTable size to 64MB, configuring Bloom filters to 10 bits per key, and allocating a block cache size of 1GB. By default, four CPU cores are assigned, with four background compaction threads, following the optimization tuning guide. We also use direct I/O to avoid the impact of the operating system’s page cache. For fairness, we limit the amount of fast devices allocated to each KV store to 10% of the dataset size (e.g., 10 GB for a 100 GB dataset). Specifically, for DecouKV, we use the configuration described in Sec 3, deploying AOFs on fast devices while controlling space usage. The initial size of the IndexTable is set to 16MB. For RocksDB and ADOC, we place L0, L1, and the WAL on fast devices, slightly increasing the sizes of L0 and L1 to ensure an equal allocation of fast devices. For MatrixKV, PrismDB and SplitDB, we follow the configuration from their paper, placing their differentiated data structures on fast devices and allocating an equivalent amount of space.

First, we evaluate the resource utilization, throughput, average latency, and tail latency of various fundamental KV operations using the following workloads: (i) inserting 100 GB KV pairs, (ii) updating 100 GB KV pairs, (iii) reading 100 GB KV pairs, and (iv) scanning 10 GB KV pairs with a maximum scan length of 100. Prior to each experiment, we clear the entire database to avoid interference. By default, we perform the microbenchmark evaluations using a uniform workload with a KV size of 1KB (24B for the key and 1000B for the value). We also conduct the same operations under a zipfian distribution and observe similar results, so we omit the results in the interest of space.
sections.translationMarkdown:
本节在混合存储设备上对 DecouKV、RocksDB v9.3.0 以及 2.3 节提到的两类解决方案进行评估和比较。在采用固定差异化管理方法的工作中，MatrixKV 是一个典型代表；在表层调度方向，ADOC 代表了当前最先进的水平。因此，我们选取 RocksDB、MatrixKV 和 ADOC 作为基本的对比基准。此外，PrismDB [42] 和 SplitDB [6] 等一些面向混合存储的最新工作主要关注负载偏斜或读密集型负载等不同场景。为确保评估全面，我们还将它们纳入 YCSB 负载下的对比分析。测试数据来自三次实验运行，各次结果之间的差异很小，文中给出其平均值。

**硬件环境。** 实验在一台配备两个 20 核 Intel Xeon Gold 5218R 处理器和 128 GB DRAM 的服务器上进行。服务器运行 Ubuntu 20.04.6 LTS，Linux 内核版本为 5.15。在存储设备方面，我们使用 128 GB Intel Optane DCPMM 和 INTEL SSDPE2KE032T8 NVMe SSD 作为快速设备，使用带 SATA 3.0 接口的 960 GB Intel S4520 SSD 作为慢速设备。

**键值存储配置。** 对于所有 KV store，我们采用推荐配置 [21]：Memtable 大小和 SSTable 大小均设为 64MB，Bloom filter 配置为每个 key 10 bit，并分配 1GB 的 block cache。按照优化调优指南，默认分配四个 CPU 核和四个后台 compaction 线程。我们还使用 direct I/O，以避免操作系统 page cache 的影响。为保证公平性，我们将分配给每个 KV store 的快速设备空间限制为数据集大小的 10%（例如，100 GB 数据集分配 10 GB）。具体而言，对于 DecouKV，我们采用第 3 节所述配置，在快速设备上部署 AOF，同时控制空间用量。IndexTable 的初始大小设为 16MB。对于 RocksDB 和 ADOC，我们将 L0、L1 和 WAL 放置在快速设备上，并略微增大 L0 和 L1 的大小，以确保分配相同容量的快速设备空间。对于 MatrixKV、PrismDB 和 SplitDB，我们遵循各自论文中的配置，将其差异化数据结构放在快速设备上，并分配等量空间。

首先，我们使用以下负载评估各种基本 KV 操作的资源利用率、吞吐量、平均延迟和尾延迟：(i) 插入 100 GB KV pair；(ii) 更新 100 GB KV pair；(iii) 读取 100 GB KV pair；(iv) 扫描 10 GB KV pair，最大扫描长度为 100。每次实验前，我们都会清空整个数据库以避免干扰。默认情况下，microbenchmark 评估采用均匀负载，KV 大小为 1KB（key 为 24B，value 为 1000B）。我们也在 Zipfian 分布下执行了相同操作，并观察到相似结果，因此出于篇幅考虑省略这些结果。

sections.title: 4.1 Microbenchmark
sections.originalMarkdown:
**Resource utilization.** We evaluate the variation in CPU and disk utilization using psutil [44] during insert and update operations over a 1500-second period. We calculate the average CPU utilization and fluctuation range during this time, as shown in Figure 11. For the insert operation, compared to RocksDB, MatrixKV, and ADOC, DecouKV achieves a 32.3%, 25.4%, and 27.3% increase in average CPU utilization, respectively, while significantly reducing the fluctuation range. This improvement is a key factor contributing to the significant write performance gains achieved by DecouKV. For the update operation, compared to RocksDB, MatrixKV, and ADOC, DecouKV improves average CPU utilization by 47.9%, 36.9%, and 39.4%, respectively. Due to the capacity limitations of fast devices, DecouKV experiences resource fluctuations. However, upon examining the fluctuation intervals, we observe that DecouKV maintains relatively stable resource utilization over extended periods.

**Figure 11:** The red solid line represents the real-time CPU utilization over a 1500-second period, measured at a granularity of one second. The black dashed line indicates the average CPU utilization over this period. In the figure, “Avg” denotes the average value, and “Rng” represents the range, reflecting the extent of fluctuation in utilization.

Figure 12 presents the average disk utilization over the same period. Compared to RocksDB, MatrixKV, and ADOC, DecouKV achieves a 17.6%–31.6% increase in disk utilization for the insert operation and an 8.0%–19.9% increase in disk utilization for the update operation. Additionally, we observe that MatrixKV exhibits high disk utilization, indicating that fine-grained compaction can mitigate intertwined resource consumption and improve disk utilization. However, due to interdependencies among operations, it still suffers from significant resource fluctuations. ADOC reduces blocking by scheduling data sorting operations, improving average resource utilization compared to RocksDB. Nonetheless, on hybrid storage devices, it experiences even more severe resource fluctuations due to intertwined resource consumption. In summary, DecouKV improves average resource utilization and reduces fragmentation by mitigating resource usage dependency.

**Figure 12:** Disk utilization.

**Throughput.** Figure 13(a) presents the throughput results for various operations. To facilitate comparison, we normalize the experimental results using the throughput of RocksDB as the reference and annotate the absolute values in the figure. Compared to RocksDB, DecouKV achieves 4.3× the throughput for inserts and 4.6× the throughput for updates. Additionally, DecouKV achieves 1.2× the throughput for reads and 1.5× the throughput for scans. Under read-intensive workloads, the background threads remain idle and DecouKV is adapted to allow a very limited number of overlapping IndexTables and files, resulting in a higher degree of data order and fewer levels in the LSM-tree (e.g., HL0 is empty in most cases). Meanwhile, benefiting from the excellent random access performance of fast storage devices, the impact of removing the Bloom Filter in AOF is effectively mitigated. As a result, DecouKV’s read performance is not significantly affected and even shows a slight improvement.

Compared to MatrixKV and ADOC, DecouKV also demonstrates a significant advantage under write-intensive workloads. Specifically, DecouKV achieves 1.6× and 2.2× the throughput of MatrixKV and ADOC, respectively, for insert operations, and 2.2× and 2.1× the throughput for update operations. Additionally, DecouKV shows good performance in both read and scan operations.

**Figure 13:** Performance under different operations.

**Average latency.** Figure 13(b) presents the experimental results normalized to DecouKV’s average latency, with DecouKV’s latency values annotated in the figure. Compared to RocksDB, DecouKV reduces the average latencies for inserts and updates by 76.0% and 77.9%, respectively. In comparison to MatrixKV and ADOC, DecouKV achieves a reduction in average latencies for inserts and updates ranging from 38.7%–56.9%. Additionally, DecouKV achieves average latencies for reads and scans comparable to those of other KV stores.

**Tail latency.** Table 1 presents the P90 and P99 tail latency results for insert and update operations. We observe that compared to RocksDB, MatrixKV, and ADOC, DecouKV reduces the P90 tail latency of the insert operation by 71.6%–83.3% and the P99 tail latency by 74.3%–91.4%. For the update operation, DecouKV reduces the P90 tail latency by 69.8%–80.4%. These improvements are attributed to the stable resource utilization and the reduction in resource bottlenecks in DecouKV. However, DecouKV does not show a significant advantage in P99 tail latency, which corroborates the fluctuations in resource utilization shown in Figure 11.

| Latency (ms) | Insert 90% | Insert 99% | Update 90% | Update 99% |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| RocksDB | 9.454 | 37.557 | 11.619 | 16.132 |
| MatrixKV | 5.571 | 12.532 | 10.010 | 22.219 |
| ADOC | 6.995 | 17.881 | 7.545 | 21.075 |
| DecouKV | 1.579 | 3.227 | 2.275 | 14.480 |

**Table 1:** Tail Latency.
sections.translationMarkdown:
**资源利用率。** 我们使用 psutil [44] 评估插入和更新操作在 1500 秒期间 CPU 和磁盘利用率的变化。我们计算了这段时间内 CPU 的平均利用率和波动范围，如图 11 所示。对于插入操作，与 RocksDB、MatrixKV 和 ADOC 相比，DecouKV 的平均 CPU 利用率分别提高了 32.3%、25.4% 和 27.3%，同时显著缩小了波动范围。该改进是 DecouKV 显著提升写性能的关键因素。对于更新操作，与 RocksDB、MatrixKV 和 ADOC 相比，DecouKV 的平均 CPU 利用率分别提高了 47.9%、36.9% 和 39.4%。受快速设备容量限制，DecouKV 会出现资源波动。然而，通过考察波动区间，我们观察到 DecouKV 能在较长时间内维持相对稳定的资源利用率。

**图 11：** 红色实线表示 1500 秒期间的实时 CPU 利用率，测量粒度为一秒。黑色虚线表示这段时间的平均 CPU 利用率。图中，“Avg”表示平均值，“Rng”表示取值范围，反映利用率的波动程度。

图 12 展示了同一时间段内的平均磁盘利用率。与 RocksDB、MatrixKV 和 ADOC 相比，DecouKV 在插入操作中的磁盘利用率提高了 17.6%–31.6%，在更新操作中的磁盘利用率提高了 8.0%–19.9%。此外，我们观察到 MatrixKV 的磁盘利用率较高，这表明细粒度 compaction 能够缓解相互交织的资源消耗并提高磁盘利用率。然而，由于操作之间存在相互依赖，它仍会出现显著的资源波动。与 RocksDB 相比，ADOC 通过调度数据排序操作减少阻塞，提高了平均资源利用率。不过，在混合存储设备上，相互交织的资源消耗使其资源波动更加严重。总之，DecouKV 通过缓解资源使用依赖提高了平均资源利用率，并减少了资源碎片化。

**图 12：** 磁盘利用率。

**吞吐量。** 图 13(a) 展示了各种操作的吞吐量结果。为了便于比较，我们以 RocksDB 的吞吐量为基准对实验结果进行归一化，并在图中标注绝对值。与 RocksDB 相比，DecouKV 的插入吞吐量达到其 4.3×，更新吞吐量达到其 4.6×。此外，DecouKV 的读取吞吐量达到其 1.2×，扫描吞吐量达到其 1.5×。在读密集型负载下，后台线程处于空闲状态，DecouKV 会自适应地只允许极少量彼此重叠的 IndexTable 和文件，从而使数据有序程度更高，LSM-tree 的层数更少（例如，大多数情况下 HL0 为空）。同时，得益于快速存储设备出色的随机访问性能，移除 AOF 中 Bloom Filter 所造成的影响得到有效缓解。因此，DecouKV 的读取性能并未受到显著影响，甚至略有提升。

与 MatrixKV 和 ADOC 相比，DecouKV 在写密集型负载下也表现出显著优势。具体而言，在插入操作中，DecouKV 的吞吐量分别达到 MatrixKV 和 ADOC 的 1.6× 和 2.2×；在更新操作中则分别达到 2.2× 和 2.1×。此外，DecouKV 在读取和扫描操作中也表现良好。

**图 13：** 不同操作下的性能。

**平均延迟。** 图 13(b) 展示了以 DecouKV 平均延迟为基准进行归一化的实验结果，并在图中标注了 DecouKV 的延迟值。与 RocksDB 相比，DecouKV 将插入和更新的平均延迟分别降低了 76.0% 和 77.9%。与 MatrixKV 和 ADOC 相比，DecouKV 将插入和更新的平均延迟降低了 38.7%–56.9%。此外，DecouKV 的读取和扫描平均延迟与其他 KV store 相当。

**尾延迟。** 表 1 给出了插入和更新操作的 P90 与 P99 尾延迟结果。我们观察到，与 RocksDB、MatrixKV 和 ADOC 相比，DecouKV 将插入操作的 P90 尾延迟降低了 71.6%–83.3%，将 P99 尾延迟降低了 74.3%–91.4%。对于更新操作，DecouKV 将 P90 尾延迟降低了 69.8%–80.4%。这些改进源于 DecouKV 稳定的资源利用率以及资源瓶颈的减少。不过，DecouKV 在 P99 尾延迟方面并未表现出显著优势，这也印证了图 11 所示的资源利用率波动。

| 延迟（ms） | 插入 90% | 插入 99% | 更新 90% | 更新 99% |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| RocksDB | 9.454 | 37.557 | 11.619 | 16.132 |
| MatrixKV | 5.571 | 12.532 | 10.010 | 22.219 |
| ADOC | 6.995 | 17.881 | 7.545 | 21.075 |
| DecouKV | 1.579 | 3.227 | 2.275 | 14.480 |

**表 1：** 尾延迟。

sections.title: 4.2 YCSB Benchmark
sections.originalMarkdown:
In this section, we evaluate DecouKV using the YCSB-C [39, 43], a C++ version of YCSB [11] benchmark. YCSB (Yahoo! Cloud Serving Benchmark) is a widely used testing tool that generates workloads based on real-world data characteristics. We conduct our evaluation using the six workload configurations shown in Table 2. Each workload runs 100 million operations on a randomly loaded 100GB database, with a KV size of 1KB (24B for the key and 1000B for the value). All other settings remain consistent with those used in previous experiments.

| Workload | Characteristics |
|---|---|
| A (Update Heavy) | 50% updates, 50% reads |
| B (Read Mostly) | 5% updates, 95% reads |
| C (Read Only) | 100% reads |
| D (Read Latest) | 5% inserts, 95% reads |
| E (Scan Mostly) | 5% inserts, 95% scans |
| F (Read-Modify-Write) | 50% read-modify-write, 50% reads |

**Table 2:** YCSB core workloads.

Figure 14(b) and Figure 14(a) present the throughput of RocksDB, MatrixKV, PrismDB, SplitDB, ADOC, and DecouKV under the YCSB uniform and zipfian workloads.

**Figure 14:** Throughput under YCSB workloads.

**Write-intensive workloads (load, YCSB-A, and -F).** For write-intensive workloads, DecouKV demonstrates significant throughput improvement. Compared to RocksDB, DecouKV achieves a 2.3–4.9× improvement. In comparison to MatrixKV and ADOC, DecouKV shows a smaller improvement under the load workload, with a 1.4× and 1.6× increase, respectively. This indicates that MatrixKV and ADOC, as optimized solutions for addressing write stall issues, effectively reduce the occurrence of write stalls under pure write workloads, leading to superior performance. However, for read-write mixed write-intensive workloads like YCSB-A and YCSB-F, the resource bottlenecks caused by frequent writes also impact read performance. DecouKV successfully mitigates resource fragmentation, reducing its impact on both read and write performance, thus providing an advantage with a 1.5–3.4× improvement. Compared to PrismDB and SplitDB, DecouKV demonstrates significant advantages under uniform workloads, achieving improvements of 1.5–3.6×. Under heavily skewed Zipfian workloads, PrismDB performs particularly well in write-intensive cases (YCSB-A, YCSB-F). This is because PrismDB employs a slab-based structure and in-place update strategy on the fast devices. When the workload is highly skewed, a large portion of write requests are handled on the fast devices and are not pushed to the slow device, thus achieving high throughput. However, DecouKV still achieves strong performance overall.

Additionally, according to our previous analysis, the system often enters a resource-constrained state under write-intensive workloads. In such cases, DecouKV relaxes certain parameters, resulting in the creation of multiple overlapping files (e.g., IndexTables or SSTables in HL0), which could impact read performance. However, in our experiments, we observe that DecouKV’s read performance is not significantly affected. This is because, under write-intensive workloads, with the scheduling of decoupled tasks in DecouKV, the burst in CPU usage is short-lived, thereby preventing excessive IndexTables (in the evaluation, the number of IndexTables never exceeds 6, which is even lower than the number of files in L0 in RocksDB, even during write stalls). As shown in Figure 16, the maximum value of IMTN is only 4, further validating this result. Regarding overlapping SSTables in HL0, we set the number of files to one in order to trigger the compaction of data from HL0 to HL1, giving HL0 compaction a higher priority. In the evaluation, even under write-intensive workloads, the number of files in HL0 does not exceed two. Under read-intensive workloads, HL0 is empty in most cases.

**Read-intensive and scan-intensive workloads (YCSB-B, -C, -D, and -E).** For read-intensive and scan-intensive workloads, DecouKV also demonstrates modest improvements. Compared to RocksDB, MatrixKV, and ADOC, DecouKV achieves a 1.2–1.9× improvement under the zipfian workload and a 1.2–2.3× improvement under the uniform workload. These gains primarily stem from more efficient resource utilization and a reduction in the number of high levels on slow devices. Compared to PrismDB and SplitDB, DecouKV also exhibits slight advantages under uniform workloads, achieving a 1.1–1.8× improvement. For highly skewed zipfian workloads, DecouKV shows no advantage, whereas SplitDB performs remarkably well. SplitDB employs a cascading skiplist index–which constructs fast paths between adjacent LSM-tree levels to reduce lookup latency–and a data promotion and demotion mechanism that migrates hot data from slower SSDs to faster NVM and cold data to SSDs, thereby optimizing read performance under skewed workloads.
sections.translationMarkdown:
本节使用 YCSB-C [39, 43] 对 DecouKV 进行评估；YCSB-C 是 YCSB [11] benchmark 的 C++ 版本。YCSB（Yahoo! Cloud Serving Benchmark）是一种广泛使用的测试工具，可根据真实数据特征生成负载。我们采用表 2 所示的六种负载配置进行评估。每种负载均在一个随机加载的 100GB 数据库上执行 1 亿次操作，KV 大小为 1KB（key 为 24B，value 为 1000B）。其他所有设置均与之前的实验保持一致。

| 负载 | 特征 |
|---|---|
| A（更新密集型） | 50% 更新，50% 读取 |
| B（以读取为主） | 5% 更新，95% 读取 |
| C（只读） | 100% 读取 |
| D（读取最新数据） | 5% 插入，95% 读取 |
| E（以扫描为主） | 5% 插入，95% 扫描 |
| F（读取-修改-写入） | 50% 读取-修改-写入，50% 读取 |

**表 2：** YCSB 核心负载。

图 14(b) 和图 14(a) 分别展示了 RocksDB、MatrixKV、PrismDB、SplitDB、ADOC 和 DecouKV 在 YCSB 均匀负载和 Zipfian 负载下的吞吐量。

**图 14：** YCSB 负载下的吞吐量。

**写密集型负载（load、YCSB-A 和 -F）。** 对于写密集型负载，DecouKV 表现出显著的吞吐量提升。与 RocksDB 相比，DecouKV 提升了 2.3–4.9×。与 MatrixKV 和 ADOC 相比，DecouKV 在 load 负载下的提升较小，分别为 1.4× 和 1.6×。这表明，作为针对 write stall 问题的优化方案，MatrixKV 和 ADOC 能在纯写负载下有效减少 write stall 的发生，从而获得较好性能。然而，对于 YCSB-A 和 YCSB-F 这类读写混合的写密集型负载，频繁写入造成的资源瓶颈也会影响读取性能。DecouKV 成功缓解了资源碎片化，降低其对读写性能的影响，从而取得 1.5–3.4× 的优势。与 PrismDB 和 SplitDB 相比，DecouKV 在均匀负载下表现出显著优势，提升幅度为 1.5–3.6×。在高度偏斜的 Zipfian 负载下，PrismDB 在写密集型场景（YCSB-A、YCSB-F）中表现尤为出色。这是因为 PrismDB 在快速设备上采用基于 slab 的结构和原地更新策略。当负载高度偏斜时，大量写请求会直接在快速设备上处理，而不会被推送到慢速设备，因此能够获得较高吞吐量。尽管如此，DecouKV 的总体性能依然强劲。

此外，根据前文分析，系统在写密集型负载下经常进入资源受限状态。在这种情况下，DecouKV 会放宽某些参数，从而产生多个相互重叠的文件（例如 IndexTable 或 HL0 中的 SSTable），这可能会影响读取性能。然而，在实验中我们观察到 DecouKV 的读取性能并未受到显著影响。这是因为，在写密集型负载下，通过 DecouKV 对解耦任务的调度，CPU 使用量的突发增长持续时间很短，从而避免产生过多 IndexTable（在评估中，即使出现 write stall，IndexTable 的数量也从未超过 6，甚至少于 RocksDB 中 L0 的文件数）。如图 16 所示，IMTN 的最大值仅为 4，进一步验证了这一结果。对于 HL0 中相互重叠的 SSTable，我们将文件数阈值设为 1，以触发数据从 HL0 到 HL1 的 compaction，使 HL0 compaction 具有更高优先级。在评估中，即使在写密集型负载下，HL0 中的文件数也不超过两个。在读密集型负载下，大多数情况下 HL0 为空。

**读密集型和扫描密集型负载（YCSB-B、-C、-D 和 -E）。** 对于读密集型和扫描密集型负载，DecouKV 同样取得了一定提升。与 RocksDB、MatrixKV 和 ADOC 相比，DecouKV 在 Zipfian 负载下提升了 1.2–1.9×，在均匀负载下提升了 1.2–2.3×。这些增益主要来自更高效的资源利用，以及慢速设备上高层数量的减少。与 PrismDB 和 SplitDB 相比，DecouKV 在均匀负载下也具有小幅优势，提升了 1.1–1.8×。对于高度偏斜的 Zipfian 负载，DecouKV 没有表现出优势，而 SplitDB 的性能非常突出。SplitDB 采用级联 skiplist 索引——在相邻 LSM-tree 层之间构建快速路径以降低查询延迟——以及数据提升和降级机制，将热数据从较慢的 SSD 迁移到更快的 NVM，并将冷数据迁回 SSD，从而优化偏斜负载下的读取性能。

sections.title: 4.3 Performance Breakdown
sections.originalMarkdown:
In our design, we consider three primary techniques to optimize system performance: decoupling, scheduling strategies, and elastic capacity for high levels. In this section, we evaluate the performance contribution of each technique to DecouKV. However, task scheduling relies on decoupling, while elastic capacity high levels depends on scheduling strategies. Consequently, we evaluate three versions of DecouKV against RocksDB: DecouKV-D (decoupling only, with the queue parameters set to the average of the auto-tuning results from the experiment), DecouKV-DS (decoupling + scheduling strategies), and DecouKV (decoupling + scheduling strategies + elastic capacity high levels, i.e., the complete version of DecouKV). Figure 15 shows the throughput of different DecouKV versions during insert and update operations, normalized to DecouKV as the baseline. In the following, we will explain the contribution of each component.

**Figure 15:** Normalized throughput of different DecouKV versions.

**Decoupling technique.** By comparing the performance of DecouKV-D with RocksDB, we can roughly estimate the contribution of the decoupling technique. The decoupling technique contributes significantly to both the load and YCSB-A workload, with a greater contribution observed under the YCSB-A workload. This is because DecouKV’s decoupling technique primarily targets data on fast devices, and a substantial portion of the updates in the YCSB-A workload can be completed solely on fast devices.

**Scheduling strategies.** Similarly, by examining the differences between DecouKV-DS and DecouKV-D, we can roughly assess that the scheduling queue technique has a greater contribution to the load workload. To further understand this, we record the variations in the parameters DFTS and IMTN during load and YCSB-A workload in the 2000s, as shown in Figure 16. After decoupling, during the load workload, DFTS is significantly increased, indicating a clear performance bottleneck in I/O. DecouKV mitigates this bottleneck by increasing DFTS to schedule more CPU-intensive operations, thus reducing the demand for I/O resources. In contrast, during the YCSB-A workload, DFTS and IMTN are not significantly adjusted, suggesting that the CPU and I/O resources are well balanced after decoupling, and no excessive scheduling is required. Therefore, the scheduling queue technique contributes more to load workload.

**Figure 16:** Tuning actions for load and YCSB-A workloads.

**Elastic capacity high levels.** The performance difference between DecouKV and DecouKV-DS demonstrates that the elastic capacity for high levels technique provides greater benefits for load workload compared to YCSB-A workload. This is primarily because the load workload has a more significant impact on the high levels than the YCSB-A workload. Moreover, the elastic capacity high levels technique further mitigates the interdependencies among operations, minimizing the impact of excessive data sorting tasks on other operations during the write-intensive load phase.
sections.translationMarkdown:
在设计中，我们采用三项主要技术来优化系统性能：解耦、调度策略以及高层弹性容量。本节评估每项技术对 DecouKV 性能的贡献。不过，任务调度依赖于解耦，而高层弹性容量又依赖于调度策略。因此，我们将三个 DecouKV 版本与 RocksDB 进行比较：DecouKV-D（仅解耦，队列参数设为实验中自动调优结果的平均值）、DecouKV-DS（解耦 + 调度策略）以及 DecouKV（解耦 + 调度策略 + 高层弹性容量，即 DecouKV 的完整版本）。图 15 展示了不同 DecouKV 版本在插入和更新操作期间的吞吐量，并以 DecouKV 为基准进行归一化。下面将说明各组件的贡献。

**图 15：** 不同 DecouKV 版本的归一化吞吐量。

**解耦技术。** 通过比较 DecouKV-D 与 RocksDB 的性能，我们可以粗略估计解耦技术的贡献。解耦技术对 load 和 YCSB-A 负载都有显著贡献，并且在 YCSB-A 负载下的贡献更大。这是因为 DecouKV 的解耦技术主要针对快速设备上的数据，而 YCSB-A 负载中的很大一部分更新只需在快速设备上即可完成。

**调度策略。** 类似地，通过考察 DecouKV-DS 与 DecouKV-D 之间的差异，我们可以粗略判断，调度队列技术对 load 负载的贡献更大。为了进一步理解这一点，我们记录了 load 和 YCSB-A 负载在 2000 秒内 DFTS 和 IMTN 参数的变化，如图 16 所示。解耦后，在 load 负载期间，DFTS 显著增大，表明 I/O 存在明显的性能瓶颈。DecouKV 通过增大 DFTS 来调度更多 CPU 密集型操作，从而降低对 I/O 资源的需求，缓解该瓶颈。相比之下，在 YCSB-A 负载期间，DFTS 和 IMTN 没有发生显著调整，这说明解耦后 CPU 与 I/O 资源已经较为均衡，无须进行过多调度。因此，调度队列技术对 load 负载的贡献更大。

**图 16：** load 和 YCSB-A 负载的调优动作。

**高层弹性容量。** DecouKV 与 DecouKV-DS 之间的性能差异表明，与 YCSB-A 负载相比，高层弹性容量技术能为 load 负载带来更大收益。主要原因是 load 负载对高层的影响比 YCSB-A 负载更显著。此外，高层弹性容量技术进一步缓解了操作之间的相互依赖，尽可能降低写密集型 load 阶段中过多数据排序任务对其他操作的影响。

sections.title: 4.4 Performance under Other Workloads
sections.originalMarkdown:
**YCSB workloads with a huge size dataset.** We conduct performance evaluations on a larger YCSB uniform workload, which includes a 500GB dataset (i.e., 500 million key-value pairs), as shown in Figure 17(a). Compared to RocksDB, DecouKV demonstrates performance improvements of 5.2×, 2.8×, and 2.4× under write-intensive load, YCSB-A, and YCSB-F workloads, respectively, consistent with the results obtained with the 100GB dataset. In comparison to MatrixKV and ADOC, DecouKV achieves performance improvements of 1.6–2.4× under the same workloads, even slightly outperforming the results from the 100GB dataset. This is attributed to the slight performance degradation observed in MatrixKV and ADOC with larger datasets, whereas DecouKV maintains its performance advantage due to more efficient utilization of system resources.

**Nutanix production workloads.** We also evaluate the throughput performance of various KV systems using a real-world workload from Nutanix, which closely resembles a write-intensive workload with 57% updates, 41% reads, and 2% scans. The results, shown in Figure 17(b), demonstrate that DecouKV outperforms other systems, achieving a throughput improvement of 1.3–2.0×.

**Figure 17:** Performance under other workloads.
sections.translationMarkdown:
**采用超大数据集的 YCSB 负载。** 我们在更大规模的 YCSB 均匀负载上进行性能评估，其中包含 500GB 数据集（即 5 亿个 key-value pair），如图 17(a) 所示。与 RocksDB 相比，DecouKV 在写密集型 load、YCSB-A 和 YCSB-F 负载下分别将性能提高了 5.2×、2.8× 和 2.4×，与 100GB 数据集上的结果一致。与 MatrixKV 和 ADOC 相比，DecouKV 在相同负载下将性能提高了 1.6–2.4×，甚至略优于 100GB 数据集上的结果。这是因为 MatrixKV 和 ADOC 在更大的数据集上出现了轻微性能下降，而 DecouKV 由于能更高效地利用系统资源，仍保持其性能优势。

**Nutanix 生产负载。** 我们还使用来自 Nutanix 的真实负载评估各种 KV 系统的吞吐量性能。该负载与写密集型负载非常相似，其中更新占 57%、读取占 41%、扫描占 2%。图 17(b) 所示结果表明，DecouKV 优于其他系统，吞吐量提高了 1.3–2.0×。

**图 17：** 其他负载下的性能。

sections.title: 4.5 Understanding DecouKV Performance
sections.originalMarkdown:
**Impact of KV size.** We evaluate the variation in throughput performance of the load under different value sizes. As shown in Figure 18(a), DecouKV demonstrates the most significant advantage when the value size is 1KB and 4KB. It is known that as the value size increases, the demand for I/O bandwidth also increases. Conversely, when the value size decreases, the load’s demand for CPU resources becomes more significant. When the value size is too large (16KB) or too small (256B), severe imbalances in the demands for CPU and I/O resources results in performance degradation, leading to resource shortages of a single type. Consequently, the benefits of decoupling decrease, and ADOC, which smooths the data flow through scheduling, still performs well.

**Impact of core number.** We evaluate the impact of the number of CPU cores on load performance. As shown in Figure 18(b), when the number of CPU cores is low (e.g., 2 cores), the performance improvement of DecouKV is modest due to limited CPU resources. As the number of cores increases, DecouKV’s performance advantage becomes more pronounced, indicating that DecouKV can better leverage the additional CPU resources. However, when CPU resources become excessive (e.g., 16 cores), the performance of DecouKV no longer increases, as it becomes constrained by disk I/O.

**Figure 18:** Performance impact of varying value sizes/core counts.
