**Performance on different devices.** We evaluate the throughput performance of DecouKV on different fast devices. Specifically, we use the INTEL SSDPE2KE032T8 NVMe SSD as the fast device and leverage io-uring to fully exploit the asynchronous I/O capabilities of the NVMe SSD. As shown in Figure 19(a), DecouKV demonstrates a throughput improvement of 1.4–2.4× over RocksDB, MatrixKV, and ADOC when deployed on hybrid storage devices consisting of an NVMe SSD and a SATA SSD.

**Memory budget.** We record the maximum memory usage during the experiments, with YCSB-A requiring the highest memory. As shown in Figure 19(b), about 23% (0.7GB) of memory is used for the IndexTable–more than RocksDB’s MemTable (0.3GB). Approximately 45% (1.4GB) is used for DecouKV’s Tablecache, less than RocksDB’s (1.56GB), because there’s no need to cache the AOF’s Filter Block and Index Block. The memory usage of DecouKV is comparable to that of MatrixKV and ADOC, with only a slight increase over RocksDB, while achieving higher performance.

**Figure 19:** Comparison of throughput after replacing fast devices with NVMe SSDs and memory budget.

**Recovery time.** We also test the recovery capability of DecouKV in the event of system crashes. Specifically, we verify that data persisted in the AOFs on fast devices before a crash can be used to rebuild the index upon reboot. The results show that DecouKV successfully recovers after multiple crash tests. In terms of performance, the recovery speed of DecouKV and RocksDB is nearly identical, with recovery times of 8.76s and 8.12s, respectively, for a 10GB database. This is because the high sequential read bandwidth provided by the fast devices enables rapid index rebuilding.
sections.translationMarkdown:
**KV 大小的影响。** 我们评估不同 value 大小下 load 吞吐量性能的变化。如图 18(a) 所示，当 value 大小为 1KB 和 4KB 时，DecouKV 的优势最为显著。众所周知，随着 value 大小增加，对 I/O 带宽的需求也会增加；相反，当 value 大小减小时，load 对 CPU 资源的需求会变得更加显著。当 value 过大（16KB）或过小（256B）时，CPU 与 I/O 资源需求的严重失衡会导致性能下降，造成某一种资源短缺。因此，解耦的收益会减小，而通过调度来平滑数据流的 ADOC 仍然表现良好。

**CPU 核数的影响。** 我们评估 CPU 核数对 load 性能的影响。如图 18(b) 所示，当 CPU 核数较少（例如 2 核）时，由于 CPU 资源有限，DecouKV 的性能提升幅度不大。随着核数增加，DecouKV 的性能优势更加明显，说明 DecouKV 能够更好地利用额外的 CPU 资源。然而，当 CPU 资源过多（例如 16 核）时，DecouKV 的性能不再提高，因为此时它受到磁盘 I/O 的限制。

**图 18：** 不同 value 大小/CPU 核数对性能的影响。

**不同设备上的性能。** 我们评估 DecouKV 在不同快速设备上的吞吐量性能。具体而言，我们使用 INTEL SSDPE2KE032T8 NVMe SSD 作为快速设备，并利用 io-uring 充分发挥 NVMe SSD 的异步 I/O 能力。如图 19(a) 所示，当部署在由 NVMe SSD 和 SATA SSD 组成的混合存储设备上时，DecouKV 的吞吐量比 RocksDB、MatrixKV 和 ADOC 高 1.4–2.4×。

**内存预算。** 我们记录了实验期间的最大内存用量，其中 YCSB-A 所需内存最多。如图 19(b) 所示，约 23%（0.7GB）的内存用于 IndexTable，超过 RocksDB 的 MemTable（0.3GB）。约 45%（1.4GB）的内存用于 DecouKV 的 Tablecache，少于 RocksDB（1.56GB），因为无须缓存 AOF 的 Filter Block 和 Index Block。DecouKV 的内存用量与 MatrixKV 和 ADOC 相当，仅略高于 RocksDB，同时实现了更高性能。

**图 19：** 将快速设备替换为 NVMe SSD 后的吞吐量对比，以及内存预算对比。

**恢复时间。** 我们还测试了 DecouKV 在系统崩溃时的恢复能力。具体而言，我们验证了崩溃前持久化在快速设备 AOF 中的数据可用于在重启时重建索引。结果表明，DecouKV 在多次崩溃测试后均能成功恢复。在性能方面，对于一个 10GB 数据库，DecouKV 和 RocksDB 的恢复速度几乎相同，恢复时间分别为 8.76s 和 8.12s。这是因为快速设备提供的高顺序读取带宽能够支持快速重建索引。

sections.title: 5 Related Work
sections.originalMarkdown:
**Performance optimization of LSM-tree-based KV stores.** A lot of research focus on optimizing the performance of LSM-tree-based KV stores. One line of investigation addresses the issue of write stalls. SILK [4] aims to mitigate the tail latencies caused by write stalls by adjusting the priority of background threads and rate-limiting their operations. ADOC identifies data overflow in various components of LSM trees as the direct cause of write stalls and fine-tunes parameters such as the number of threads and batch size to schedule flush and compaction operations more effectively. Another line of research focuses on reducing write amplification through KV separation. WiscKey [35] is the first to propose storing values in a separate append-only log, while Bourbon [12] extends WiscKey by indexing values through a learned approach. HashKV [8] and BadgerDB [16] have optimized the additional overhead introduced by garbage collection in the context of KV separation. Other studies focus on optimizing read and scan performance, including systems such as LSM-trie [46], UniKV [49], ElasticBF [34], and DiffKV [33].

**Deploying LSM-tree-based KV stores on new devices.** Recent studies also focus on new types of storage media, with some research targeting hybrid storage solutions, similar to the scenario discussed in this paper. NoveLSM extends memory components to PM and employs in-place updates to reduce compaction overhead. SLM-DB [28] improves upon this by constructing a global B+-tree index on PM and using optional compaction to minimize write amplification. MatrixKV identifies L0-L1 compaction as the root cause of PM device write stalls and addresses this issue by using matrix containers for differentiated management of L0 on PM. Fine-grained matrix compression is employed to reduce write stalls.

Besides, some works [6, 42, 45] mainly focus on different scenarios like workload skewness or read-intensive workloads. PrismDB modifies the hierarchical structure to reduce compaction overhead and uses a cold-hot awareness approach to improve read performance. SplitDB employs a cascading skip list index and a data promotion and demotion mechanism that migrates hot data from slower SSDs to faster NVM and cold data back to SSDs, thereby optimizing read performance under skewed workloads. PRISM [45] analyzes the advantages of each storage device and adopts hybrid management for heterogeneous storage. However, abandoning the tree structure leads to some loss in scan performance and complicates garbage collection management. Other studies focus on scenarios where cost is not a primary concern, and fast devices nearly replace slow ones. KVell [32] relaxes data ordering and employs a share-nothing design to fully utilize the bandwidth of fast devices. ListDB [30] deploys the entire LSM-tree on a system with only DRAM and PM and replaces SSTables with a NUMA-aware skip list. MioDB [17] leverages the byte-addressable feature of PM, managing data on PM using skip lists and performing zero-copy compaction for merging between skip lists.

**Tuning and scheduling of LSM-tree-based KV stores.** Many studies observe that configuration settings significantly impact the performance of LSM-trees. Monkey [13] proposed a configuration tuning framework that optimizes memory allocation strategies and read/write performance under worst-case scenarios. Dostoevsky [14] employs a hybrid strategy to determine the levels at which compaction occurs. Rafiki [37] and TiKV [41] utilize offline training methods to compute optimal configuration settings. These works achieve substantial performance improvements in their respective scenarios; however, their tuning and scheduling approaches are often highly dependent on workload characteristics.
sections.translationMarkdown:
**基于 LSM-tree 的 KV store 性能优化。** 大量研究致力于优化基于 LSM-tree 的 KV store 性能。其中一类研究关注 write stall 问题。SILK [4] 通过调整后台线程的优先级并对其操作进行限速，力图缓解 write stall 导致的尾延迟。ADOC 将 LSM-tree 各组件中的数据溢出确定为 write stall 的直接原因，并对线程数、batch size 等参数进行微调，从而更有效地调度 flush 和 compaction 操作。另一类研究关注通过 KV separation 降低写放大。WiscKey [35] 最早提出将 value 存储在单独的 append-only log 中，而 Bourbon [12] 在 WiscKey 的基础上采用 learned 方法为 value 建立索引。HashKV [8] 和 BadgerDB [16] 对 KV separation 场景下垃圾回收引入的额外开销进行了优化。还有一些研究关注优化读取和扫描性能，包括 LSM-trie [46]、UniKV [49]、ElasticBF [34] 和 DiffKV [33] 等系统。

**在新型设备上部署基于 LSM-tree 的 KV store。** 近期研究也开始关注新型存储介质，其中一些工作与本文讨论的场景类似，面向混合存储方案。NoveLSM 将内存组件扩展到 PM，并采用原地更新来降低 compaction 开销。SLM-DB [28] 在此基础上进行改进，在 PM 上构建全局 B+-tree 索引，并利用可选 compaction 尽可能降低写放大。MatrixKV 将 L0-L1 compaction 确定为 PM 设备发生 write stall 的根本原因，并通过使用矩阵容器对 PM 上的 L0 进行差异化管理来解决该问题。它采用细粒度矩阵压缩来减少 write stall。

此外，一些工作 [6, 42, 45] 主要关注负载偏斜或读密集型负载等不同场景。PrismDB 修改层次结构来降低 compaction 开销，并使用冷热感知方法提高读取性能。SplitDB 采用级联 skip list 索引以及数据提升和降级机制，将热数据从较慢的 SSD 迁移到更快的 NVM，并将冷数据迁回 SSD，从而优化偏斜负载下的读取性能。PRISM [45] 分析每种存储设备的优势，并对异构存储采用混合管理。不过，放弃树形结构会损失一部分扫描性能，也会使垃圾回收管理更加复杂。另一些研究关注成本并非首要问题、快速设备几乎替代慢速设备的场景。KVell [32] 放宽数据有序性要求，并采用 share-nothing 设计来充分利用快速设备的带宽。ListDB [30] 将整棵 LSM-tree 部署在仅含 DRAM 和 PM 的系统上，并用 NUMA-aware skip list 替代 SSTable。MioDB [17] 利用 PM 的字节可寻址特性，通过 skip list 管理 PM 上的数据，并采用 zero-copy compaction 合并 skip list。

**基于 LSM-tree 的 KV store 调优与调度。** 许多研究观察到，配置设置会显著影响 LSM-tree 的性能。Monkey [13] 提出了一种配置调优框架，在最坏情况场景下优化内存分配策略和读写性能。Dostoevsky [14] 采用混合策略来确定在哪些层执行 compaction。Rafiki [37] 和 TiKV [41] 利用离线训练方法计算最优配置。这些工作在各自场景中实现了显著的性能提升；不过，其调优和调度方法往往高度依赖负载特征。

sections.title: 6 Conclusion
sections.originalMarkdown:
In this work, we analyze the data sorting operations of sorting-based KV stores and identify operation coupling as the root cause of resource usage dependency and fragmentation on hybrid storage devices. While two existing approaches—fixed differentiated data management and superficial scheduling of data sorting operations—fail to fundamentally address the problem of inefficient resource utilization.

We propose DecouKV to mitigate resource usage dependency in sorting-based KV stores on hybrid storage devices via operation decoupling. Experimental results demonstrate that, compared to RocksDB and the aforementioned solutions, DecouKV improves CPU utilization by 25.4%–32.3% and increases throughput by 2.3–4.9× under write-intensive workloads, while reducing tail latency by 74.3%–91.4%. Furthermore, under read-intensive workloads, DecouKV also achieves a modest throughput improvement of 1.2-2.3×.
sections.translationMarkdown:
在本文中，我们分析了基于排序的 KV store 中的数据排序操作，并确定操作耦合是混合存储设备上资源使用依赖和碎片化的根本原因。现有的两种方法——固定的差异化数据管理和对数据排序操作的表层调度——都无法从根本上解决资源利用效率低下的问题。

我们提出 DecouKV，通过操作解耦来缓解混合存储设备上基于排序的 KV store 中的资源使用依赖。实验结果表明，与 RocksDB 和上述解决方案相比，在写密集型负载下，DecouKV 将 CPU 利用率提高了 25.4%–32.3%，将吞吐量提高了 2.3–4.9×，同时将尾延迟降低了 74.3%–91.4%。此外，在读密集型负载下，DecouKV 也取得了 1.2-2.3× 的适度吞吐量提升。
